JCodesMore/ai-website-cloner-template.content: # AI Website Cloner Template A reusable template for reverse-engineering any website into a clean, modern Next.js codebase using AI coding agents. **Recommended: [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) with Opus 4.7 for best results** вҖ” but works with a variety of AI coding agents. Point it at a URL, run `/clone-website`, and your AI agent will inspect the site, extract design tokens and assets, write component specs, and dispatch parallel builders to reconstruct every section. ## Demo [![Watch the demo](docs/design-references/comparison.png)](https://youtu.be/O669pVZ_qr0) > Click the image above to watch the full demo on YouTube. ## Quick Start > **Important:** Start by making your own copy with GitHub's **Use this template** button. Do not clone this template repository directly for your website project, and do not open pull requests here with your generated website. 1. **Create your own repository from this template** On the GitHub page for this project, click **Use this template**, then click **Create a new repository**. Give your new repository a name, choose whether it should be public or private, then click **Create repository**. If GitHub shows an **Include all branches** option, you can leave it off. This gives you your own separate project to work in, so your website changes stay in your account instead of coming back to the main template. 2. **Open your new repository on your computer** After GitHub creates your copy, open that new repository. Click **Code** and open or clone your new repository with your preferred coding tool. If you use the terminal, the command will look like this: ```bash git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/YOUR-NEW-REPOSITORY.git cd YOUR-NEW-REPOSITORY ``` 3. **Install dependencies** ```bash npm install ``` 4. **Start your AI agent** вҖ” Claude Code recommended: ```bash claude --chrome ``` 5. **Run the skill**: ``` /clone-website [ ...] ``` 6. **Customize** (optional) вҖ” after the base clone is built, modify as needed > Using a different agent? Open `AGENTS.md` for project instructions вҖ” most agents pick it up automatically. ## Supported Platforms | Agent | Status | | ------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | | [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) |вҖҰ

JimLiu/baoyu-design.content: # baoyu-design **Run Claude Design on your own local agent вҖ” Cursor, Claude Code, Claude Desktop, or any fileвҖ‘capable coding agent.** [English](README.md) В· [з®ҖдҪ“дёӯж–Ү](README.zh-CN.md) В· [Changelog](CHANGELOG.md) ![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg) ![Best with Opus 4.8](https://img.shields.io/badge/Best%20with-Opus%204.8-d97757) ![Harness](https://img.shields.io/badge/Cursor%20%C2%B7%20Claude%20Code%20%C2%B7%20Codex-supported-1f6feb) `baoyu-design` packages **Claude Design** вҖ” the design engine behind [claude.ai/design](https://claude.ai/design) вҖ” as a portable **Agent Skill**. Drop it into a local agent and you get most of what the website does, right inside your editor: polished UI mockups, interactive prototypes, wireframes, landing pages, dashboards, mobile apps, and slide decks вҖ” all produced as selfвҖ‘contained HTML. No website, no separate subscription, no upload step. The agent already on your machine does the work, and every artifact stays in your repo. --- ## Screenshots | Cursor | Codex | Claude | |---|---|---| | | | | --- ## Why run it locally - **Free yourself from the website.** You get the vast majority of `claude.ai/design`'s capabilities without ever leaving your editor вҖ” same methodology, same craft standards, same output format. - **Best with Opus 4.8.** The skill is a long, demanding design brief; the stronger the model, the better the result. Pair it with **Claude Opus 4.8** for the best output, and it still works well on other capable models. - **Iterate by pointing, not describing.** Because the deliverable is plain HTML served on `localhost`, you can lean on your agent's builtвҖ‘in browser preview and elementвҖ‘annotation tools (Cursor Browser / DevTools, Claude Preview, or Codex Browser). Point at a button in the live preview, say what you want changed, and the agent edits the underlying source вҖ” a tight, visual secondвҖ‘pass editing loop that's hard to get on a website. - **Everything is yours.** Output lands in `designs//` as selfвҖ‘contained HTML you can version, fork, export, or ship. --- ## What it can make The skill drives a full design process вҖ” clarifying questions вҶ’ gathering design context вҶ’ producing one or more HTML deliverables вҶ’ previewing and verifying. It ships **24 builtвҖ‘in skills** and a set of readyвҖ‘made component scaffolds. | Area | BuiltвҖ‘in skills | |---|---| | **Core design** | HiвҖ‘fi design В· Interactive prototype В· Wireframe В· Frontend aesthetic direction | | **Decks** | Make a deck В· Speaker notes | | **Mobile & motion** | Mobile prototype В· Animated video В· Sound effects | | **Design systems** | Create design system В· Design Components (`.dc.html`) В· MakeвҖҰ

CMC-27/Vibe-App-Wiki.content:
# Vibe App Wiki рҹҡҖ

Welcome to the **Vibe App Wiki** repository. This workspace serves as a standardized, app-agnostic scaffold designed to facilitate seamless agentic development, structured planning, and comprehensive documentation management for any codebase.

---

## рҹ“Ӯ Repository Structure

Below is an overview of the core entry points and directories in this workspace:

*   **[`AGENT.md`](AGENT.md)**: The mandatory landing page and instructions for AI agents. It maps tasks to documentation files, defines development rules, and outlines the mandatory wrap-up protocol.
*   **[`HOW-TO.md`](HOW-TO.md)**: A guide detailing the agentic development lifecycle, covering setup/bootstrapping, planning methodologies (such as stateless *Pass the Parcel* or multi-stage pipelines), and pre-deployment validation.
*   **[`DESIGN.md`](DESIGN.md)**: Standard frontend design rules and CSS token references to ensure consistent, premium UI development.
*   **`/Docs`**: The structured intelligence hub of the project, split into thematic subdirectories:
    *   `core/`: Core architecture, vision, design systems, and state context.
    *   `features/`: Feature-specific logic, layouts, and components.
    *   `components/`: Catalog of design system component specifications.
    *   `database/` & `logic/`: Schema definitions and core utility standards.
    *   `logs/`: Development history records, including the [`agent-changelog.md`](Docs/logs/agent-changelog.md).

---

## рҹӣ пёҸ How to Use This Template

1.  **Configure the Vision**: Run the `create-app-vision-north-star` skill (or edit [`Docs/core/01-vision-north-star.md`](Docs/core/01-vision-north-star.md)) to define your new application's core goals.
2.  **Define the Structure**: Build out the schemas, routes, and features in the `/Docs` directory to establish a clear architectural layout before writing code.
3.  **Coordinate with AI Agents**: Direct incoming agents to read [`AGENT.md`](AGENT.md) first to ensure they adhere to the project's styling tokens, code hygiene rules, and validation pipelines.

zenhosta/9drive.content:
![9Drive cover](https://i.ibb.co.com/35BySv1C/image.png)

# 9Drive

9Drive is a storage gateway web app for connecting multiple Google Drive accounts into one virtual storage dashboard. Users can register with email/password or Google, automatically connect their first Google Drive account during Google sign-in, track quota, upload files into a dedicated `9drive` Drive folder, organize files with virtual folders, preview files, sync MySQL from Google Drive, and let the backend route uploads to the Drive account with enough free space.

## Features

- React + Vite frontend.
- Express + TypeScript backend.
- MySQL database with Prisma migrations.
- Bearer token authentication.
- Email/password auth plus Google sign-in/register with automatic first Drive connection.
- Global Google OAuth config stored encrypted in DB.
- Optional reCAPTCHA on email/password registration.
- Direct upload stream to Google Drive. Files are not stored on the server.
- Google Drive uploads are stored under a root `9drive` folder.
- Manual sync from the Google Drive `9drive` folder back into MySQL.
- Multi-account storage quota summary.
- Quota tracker page.
- Virtual folders.
- File preview, download, rename, move, and delete actions.
- Bottom-right upload progress panel.

## Preview

Live preview: https://9drive.zenhosta.com

![9Drive dashboard preview](https://i.ibb.co.com/HLjG3JRf/image.png)

![9Drive shared file preview](https://i.ibb.co.com/QLpYGmx/image.png)

## Star History

[![Star History Chart](https://api.star-history.com/svg?repos=zenhosta/9drive&type=Date)](https://www.star-history.com/#zenhosta/9drive&Date)

## Project Structure

```txt
backend/   Express API, Prisma schema, Google Drive integration
frontend/  Vite React app
```

## Requirements

- Node.js 20+
- npm
- MySQL running locally
- Google Cloud project
- Google OAuth Client ID and Client Secret

Default database used by this project:

```txt
host: localhost
port: 3306
database: 9drive
user: root
password: empty
```

## 1. Clone And Install

```bash
git clone git@github.com:zenhosta/9drive.git
cd 9drive
```

Install backend dependencies:

```bash
cd backend
npm install
```

Install frontend dependencies:

```bash
cd ../frontend
npm install
```

## 2. Create MySQL Database

Create database:

```sql
CREATE DATABASE 9drive;
```

If using MySQL CLI:

```bash
mysql -u root -e "CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS 9drive;"
```

## 3. Backend Environment

Create `backend/.env`:

```env
DATABASE_URL="mysql://root@localhost:3306/9drive"
APP_PORT=4000
FRONTEND_URL="http://localhost:5173"
JWT_ACCESS_SECRET="change-this-jwt-secret-at-least-32-chars"
TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY="change-this-encryption-key-32bytes!"
ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS=900
REFRESH_TOKEN_TTL_DAYS=30
MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES=5368709120
RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY=""

# Used only by `npm run seed:google-config`.
# These values are encrypted and stored in DB as global Google OAuth config.
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=""
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=""

# AlignDev ### Frontend conventions generator for the AI coding era В· Generate team standards and SKILL.md in one flow **Build with AI. Ship with standards.** A 7-step visual wizard that generates a complete Markdown standards document and a `SKILL.md` that Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and other coding agents can read directly. [![Next.js](https://img.shields.io/badge/Next.js-16.2-000000?logo=next.js&logoColor=white)](https://nextjs.org) [![React](https://img.shields.io/badge/React-19.2-149ECA?logo=react&logoColor=white)](https://react.dev) [![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-5.x-3178C6?logo=typescript&logoColor=white)](https://www.typescriptlang.org) [![Tailwind CSS](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tailwind%20CSS-v4-38BDF8?logo=tailwindcss&logoColor=white)](https://tailwindcss.com) [![shadcn/ui](https://img.shields.io/badge/shadcn%2Fui-latest-000000)](https://ui.shadcn.com) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-22c55e)](#-license) [![PRs Welcome](https://img.shields.io/badge/PRs-welcome-ec4899)](#-contributing)
--- ## рҹ“– About AlignDev **AlignDev** is a **frontend conventions generator** built for the AI coding era. When teams use Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Windsurf, and other AI agents at the same time, the biggest pain point is consistency: **each agent writes code based on its own interpretation, so directory structure, naming, state management, and UI style drift apart**. AlignDev turns team conventions into machine-readable and human-editable standards so every agent works from the same contract. In about 3 minutes, you can get: - A complete Markdown standards document you can **share with your team** - A `SKILL.md` you can **place in the repository root** so AI agents can load it automatically --- ## вңЁ Features | Module | Capability | Details | | --- | --- | --- | | рҹҺҜ **7-step visual wizard** | Core stack / UI / state / toolchain / directories / naming / tokens | Preview selections instantly, no config file required | | рҹ§  **Multi-framework support** | Next.js В· React (Vite) В· Vue В· Nuxt В· SvelteKit | Options adapt to the selected framework to avoid invalid combinations | | рҹҺЁ **49 UI styles** | minimalism / glassmorphism / neumorphism / brutalism / aurora / cyberpunk вҖҰ | Each style includes preset Design Tokens | | рҹҢҲ **Live Design Tokens preview** | Color depth (3 / 5 tiers), spacing (4px/8px), type scale (four ratios) | Visualize instantly and export as CSS Variables / JS Object | | вҷҝ **WCAG contrast checks** | Automatically calculates foreground/background contrast ratios | Built in `lib/wcag-utils.ts`, with AA / AAA level hints | | рҹ“Ұ **Live npm version sync** | Fetches latest major versions for 30+ mainstream packages via `app/api/versions` | Falls back to `FALLBACK_VERSIONS` on failure | | рҹ“„ **Two output artifacts** | Full standards document + `SKILL.md` | Copy or download in one click | | рҹӨ– **AI-agent frвҖҰ

zarazhangrui/beautiful-feishu-whiteboard.content:
# Beautiful Feishu Whiteboard [дёӯж–Ү README](./README.zh.md) **A library of 35 curated colour palette styles for building gorgeous, *editable* Feishu / Lark (йЈһд№Ұ) whiteboards.** See the [Feishu doc version of this intro](https://bytedance.us.larkoffice.com/docx/AldcdypvjoEqQDxcSVeu37ixs0d) (with usage-scenario showcases). This is an agent **skill** (for Claude Code and other agents that read `SKILL.md`). It is not an auto layout chart tool. Your agent composes the layout, and these templates give it a tasteful **colour palette and mood** plus the whiteboard medium's hard rendering rules. The result is a real, **editable** Feishu whiteboard inside a doc, not a screenshot. > Built from hard won, on board verified knowledge of what the Feishu SVG whiteboard renderer can and > cannot do (native shapes only, no opacity, the text colour export quirk, and more), all captured in > [`RULES.md`](RULES.md). ## Gallery Every style renders the **same content**, the three stages of LLM training, so you can compare palettes directly. The 35 styles are grouped from **restrained** to **bold**. Click a name to open its template. ### Restrained

Avocado Press
Grove

Jade Lens
Long Table

Macchiato
Monochrome

Papier Bleu
Reading Room

Salmon Stamp
### Balanced

Albert-Weasker/niubi_guard.content: # Niubi Guard A free, open-source defense system that protects GitHub maintainers from spam, harassment, and coordinated abuse. [Apache-2.0 License](./LICENSE) В· [Homepage](#web-ui) В· [GitHub](https://github.com/Albert-Weasker/niubi_guard) В· [English](./README.md) В· [з®ҖдҪ“дёӯж–Ү](./README.zh-CN.md) [What it does](#what-it-does) В· [Install](#install) В· [Web UI](#web-ui) В· [AI Detection](#ai-detection) В· [Configuration](#configuration) В· [CLI](#cli) В· [Contributing](#contributing) Niubi Guard helps maintainers defend their repositories without hiding the policy. You choose the detection signals, users, allowlists, model, prompts, confidence threshold, and response actions. Dry-run is the default. Strong actions only happen when you configure them and run apply mode. We built it because maintainers reported coordinated attacks: hostile Issues, repeated copy-paste accusations, and reputation-pressure campaigns. More maintainers are seeing the same pattern. Normal project promotion is allowed. Coordinated harassment is not. > **Don't want to self-host?** Use the free hosted version at [niubistar.com/guard](https://www.niubistar.com/guard) вҖ” no configuration needed. The open-source version is continuously improved. Issues and feedback are welcome on [GitHub](https://github.com/Albert-Weasker/niubi_guard/issues). ## What it does **Transparent.** Every detection carries labels, matched keywords or usernames, AI confidence, reasons, evidence, and planned actions. **User-controlled.** Delete, close, lock, block, and interaction-limit actions stay off until the maintainer explicitly enables them. **AI-powered.** Use your own OpenAI-compatible model. Bring your own base URL, API key, model, prompt, and confidence threshold. **Open source.** The defense logic, UI, CLI, configuration schema, and placeholder brand assets are available for maintainers to inspect and improve. **Multilingual.** The first release supports English and з®ҖдҪ“дёӯж–Ү in the web UI and documentation. ## Install Install the CLI from npm: ```bash npm install -g niubi-guard niubi-guard init niubi-guard scan --config guard.config.json ``` Or run from source: ```bash git clone https://github.com/Albert-Weasker/niubi_guard.git cd niubi_guard pnpm install ``` Run the web UI: ```bash pnpm dev:web ``` Then open `http://localhost:3000`. If that port is busy, Next.js will choose another port. Run a CLI dry-run: ```bash export GITHUB_TOKEN=github_pat_xxx pnpm dev -- init pnpm scan -- --config guard.config.json ``` Run with Docker: ```bash docker build -t niubi-guard . docker run --rm -p 3000:3000 niubi-guard ``` ## Web UI The UI is a product console and policy builder: - GitHub token and repository list - detection signals and username defense - allow phrases and allow users - OpenAI-compatible AI detection - confidence threshold and prompt editing - review-only or auto-plan mode - dry-run or apply вҖҰ

openai/role-specific-plugins.content:
# Role-Specific Plugins

Role-specific plugins make Codex easier to customize for a team's day-to-day work.
These templates package domain-specific skills, connector bindings, and starter
assets so teams can adapt Codex for roles like sales, data analytics, product
design, and financial markets. They were built with OpenAI subject matter
experts around workflows that are already helping teams move faster internally
and with alpha partners. Over the coming weeks, we'll continue expanding this
collection with more roles, workflows, and examples.

The plugins are intended to be customized before use. Connector-backed plugins
may include placeholder app and connector ids that must be replaced with ids
available to the target workspace.

## Included Plugins

| Plugin | Description | Connectors |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Sales](./plugins/sales) | Prepare for meetings, follow up after calls, review pipeline, find account context, and build deal plans. | Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, Google Drive, Gmail, Outlook, Outreach, Clay, ZoomInfo, and other sales tools |
| [Data Analytics](./plugins/data-analytics) | Query, visualize, explain, and validate datasets; build dashboards; and investigate metrics. | Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, Hex, Amplitude, Mixpanel, Statsig, Metabase, ThoughtSpot, Google Drive, Slack, Microsoft 365, and more |
| [Product Design](./plugins/product-design) | Create product specs, prototypes, UI critiques, and product design artifacts. | Sites |
| [Financial Markets](./plugins/financial-markets) | Build public-equity research, earnings analysis, valuation work, model updates, long/short pitches, risk reviews, dashboards, and investment memos. | FactSet, LSEG, Morningstar, Daloopa, Quartr, S&P, PitchBook, Slack, Google Drive, Gmail, SharePoint, Teams, and more |

## Repository Layout

```text
.
|-- .agents/plugins/marketplace.json
`-- plugins/
    |-- sales/
    |-- data-analytics/
    |-- product-design/
    `-- financial-markets/
```

Each plugin generally follows this structure:

```text
plugins/plugin-name/
|-- .codex-plugin/plugin.json   # Plugin manifest and display metadata
|-- .app.json                   # App and connector bindings, when needed
|-- .mcp.json                   # MCP server configuration, when needed
|-- skills/                     # Workflow instructions and domain context
|-- assets/                     # Icons, templates, and examples
`-- README.md                   # Plugin-specific notes, when present
```

## Configure Connectors

Connector-backed plugins declare app bindings in `.app.json`. Some bindings use
placeholder ids:

```json
{
  "apps": {
    "salesforce": {
      "id": "REPLACE_WITH_SALESFORCE_APP_OR_CONNECTOR_ID"
    }
  }
}
```

Before installing a connector-backed plugin, replace each placeholder with a
matching app or connector id available to your workspace.

| Placeholder | Replace with |
| --- | --- |
| `REPLACE_WITH_SALESFORCE_APP_OR_CONNECTOR_ID` | Salesforce or Agentforce Sales |
| `REPвҖҰ

zaydmulani09/mnemo.content:
# mnemo

> Local-first AI memory layer for any LLM. Persistent knowledge graph,
> entity extraction, semantic retrieval вҖ” no cloud required.

![Build Status](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/zaydmulani09/mnemo/ci.yml?branch=main)
![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue)
![Crates.io](https://img.shields.io/crates/v/mnemo-core)
![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/mnemo-sdk)
![Docker](https://img.shields.io/docker/pulls/zaydmulani09/mnemo)

---

## What is mnemo?

LLM apps built on custom pipelines have no persistent memory between
sessions. mnemo is a local sidecar that extracts entities, builds a
knowledge graph, and injects scored context back into your prompts вҖ”
no cloud, no Python runtime, no vendor lock-in.

mnemo is a sidecar service that watches every conversation you feed it, extracts named entities and relationships using an LLM, builds a persistent knowledge graph in SQLite, and injects relevant context back into future prompts вҖ” automatically, in under 50ms. It works with **Ollama** (fully local, free), OpenAI, Anthropic, or any OpenAI-compatible API. It ships as a single static binary with zero cloud dependency.

---

## How it works

```
  your app
     в”Ӯ
     в–ј
  POST /ingest в”Җв”Җв–ә entity extraction (LLM) в”Җв”Җв–ә knowledge graph (SQLite + petgraph)
                                                        в”Ӯ
  POST /retrieve в—„в”Җв”Җ scoring + ranking в—„в”Җв”Җ graph traversal + full-text search
     в”Ӯ
     в–ј
  context_prompt  в”Җв”Җв–ә inject into your LLM prompt
```

1. You POST raw text to `/ingest` (a conversation turn, a document, a note).
2. mnemo sends it to your configured LLM and extracts entities (people, tools, places, concepts) and the relationships between them.
3. Entities are deduplicated by name+type, aliases are merged, and everything is written to SQLite. The in-memory petgraph is updated atomically.
4. On POST `/retrieve`, mnemo runs a 6-stage pipeline: full-text chunk search вҶ’ entity name search вҶ’ graph expansion (BFS over the knowledge graph) вҶ’ relation filter вҶ’ score+rank вҶ’ assemble a `context_prompt` string.
5. You inject `context_prompt` into your LLM's system prompt. Done.

---

## Why mnemo

There are a lot of AI memory tools. Here's what makes mnemo different:

| | mnemo | Most alternatives |
|---|---|---|
| **Runtime** | Single Rust binary | Python daemon |
| **Storage** | SQLite, survives restarts | In-memory or cloud |
| **Graph layer** | petgraph, multi-hop traversal | None |
| **Cloud dependency** | Zero | Required or optional |
| **LLM backend** | Any OpenAI-compatible | Often locked to one |
| **Retrieval** | Scored + ranked, graph-expanded | Naive context dump |

**mnemo is not for everyone.** If you're using a managed agent
harness that handles memory for you, you don't need it. mnemo
is for developers building custom LLM pipelines who need
persistent, structured, local memory they fully control.

The graph layer is the real differentiator вҖ” entities are
deduplicated across sessions, rвҖҰ

--- **latte** is a lightweight, ultra-fast video and audio downloader for macOS that lives entirely in your menu bar. Built natively with SwiftUI, it allows you to download media from thousands of popular websites instantlyвҖ”without opening a browser tab, dealing with ads, or navigating complex command-line tools. --- ## рҹҸ—пёҸ Features - **Menu bar native** вҖ” Lives quietly in your menu bar. No Dock icon, no persistent window, no interruption to your workflow. - **Universal support** вҖ” Supports downloading from over 1,000+ popular platforms including YouTube, Twitch, TikTok, Vimeo, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, and major news/sports outlets. - **Browser Authentication** вҖ” Securely inject cookies from Chrome, Firefox, Brave, or Edge to download age-restricted, private, or login-required content. - **Batch downloading** вҖ” Paste multiple links at once. The UI automatically collapses to save space while fetching massive playlists or batches in the background. - **Advanced format management** вҖ” Reorder your preferred video and audio formats (supports modern codecs like AV1, WebM, MKV, FLAC, ALAC). **Show/hide formats** you don't use to keep your interface perfectly clean. - **Smart Auto-Dismiss & Pinning** вҖ” Click anywhere outside the app to hide it instantly, or use the "Keep Window Open" toggle to pin it to your screen. - **Post-processing made easy** вҖ” Automatically embed thumbnails, inject metadata, and burn subtitles directly into your downloaded files. - **Zero-dependency setup** вҖ” Automatically configures its own fast backend engine (`yt-dlp`) locally. No manual Python or terminal setup required. - **Premium ambient design** вҖ” Features a stunning, zero-overhead animated frosted glass interface (choose between Default, Rare Jade, Deep Ocean, or Floral themes). --- ## Requirements - macOS 14 (Sonoma) or later. - **ffmpeg** is recommended for merging high-quality video/audio formats and converting to specific audio types. - Install via Homebrew: `brew instalвҖҰ

rednote-hilab/dots.tts.content: **dots.tts** is a **2B-parameter fully continuous, end-to-end autoregressive (AR) text-to-speech system**. The backbone pairs a semantic encoder, an LLM, and an autoregressive flow-matching acoustic head over a **48 kHz** AudioVAE, with no discrete tokens anywhere in the pipeline. dots.tts achieves the best average performance on **Seed-TTS-Eval**, with WERs of **0.94% / 1.30% / 6.60%** and SIM scores of **81.0 / 77.1 / 79.5** on the zh / en / zh-hard test sets, respectively. It further attains the **highest average speaker similarity (83.9)** on the 24-language **MiniMax multilingual** benchmark. Across other benchmarks, dots.tts also consistently demonstrates **open-source state-of-the-art performance**, exhibiting strong generation stability, voice cloning ability, and emotional expressiveness. ### News * **[2026.06]** рҹ”Ҙ We have released **dots.tts** вҖ” 2B fully continuous AR TTS, with pretrained / self-corrective-aligned / MeanFlow-distilled checkpoints and full inference & fine-tuning code under Apache-2.0. --- ## Contents - [Quick Start](#-quick-start) - [Installation](#installation) - [CLI](#cli) - [Python API](#python-api) - [Web Demo (Gradio)](#web-demo-gradio) - [Fine-tuning](#fine-tuning) - [Architecture](#-architecture) - [Performance](#-performance) - [Seed-TTS-Eval](#seed-tts-eval) - [MiniMax Multilingual](#minimax-multilingual-24-languages) - [CV3-Eval](#cv3-eval) - [EmergentTTS-Eval](#emergenttts-eval) - [Risks and Limitations](#%EF%B8%8F-risks-and-limitations) - [Citation](#-citation) - [License](#-license) --- ## рҹҡҖ Quick Start ### Installation We recommend creating a fresh conda environment first (Python 3.10вҖ“3.12): ```bash conda create -n dots_tts python=3.10 -y conda activate dots_tts ``` Then install from source: ```bash python -m pip install --upgrade pip python -m pip install -e . -c constraints/recommended.txt ``` For training / linting extras: ```bash python -m pip install -e .[full] -c constraints/recommended.txt ``` The constraints file pins the recommended versions. To use other compatible versions, omit `-c constraints/recommended.txt`; the cвҖҰ

supperzl/ita.content: # Idp Team Automation зӢ¬з«Ӣ Python йЎ№зӣ®пјҡйҖҡиҝҮ IDP з”ҹжҲҗиҙҰеҸ·пјҢиө°зәҜ HTTP еҚҸи®®е®ҢжҲҗ ChatGPT SSO / Codex OAuthпјҢиҺ·еҸ– Codex refresh tokenпјҢе№¶еҶҷе…Ҙ Sub2APIгҖӮ ## йЎ№зӣ®з®Җд»Ӣ Idp Team Automation жҳҜдёҖдёӘеҹәдәҺ OpenAI SSO Bug зҡ„ Team жҲҗе‘ҳиҙҰеҸ·ејҖйҖҡиҮӘеҠЁеҢ–е·Ҙе…·гҖӮ йЎ№зӣ®дјҡиҮӘеҠЁе®ҢжҲҗ IDP иҙҰеҸ·з”ҹжҲҗгҖҒTeam жҲҗе‘ҳиҙҰеҸ·ејҖйҖҡгҖҒCodex жҺҲжқғ URL з”ҹжҲҗгҖҒrefresh token иҺ·еҸ–пјҢе№¶е°ҶиҙҰеҸ·еҪ•е…Ҙ Sub2APIгҖӮ IDP API зӮ№ж•°йңҖиҰҒиҒ”зі» @иҜҘйҡҗ иҙӯд№°гҖӮ ## дҪңиҖ…дҝЎжҒҜ - iDP еҚҸи®®дҪңиҖ…пјҡ@иҜҘйҡҗ - жіЁеҶҢжңәдҪңиҖ…пјҡ@жңҙеңЈдҪ‘ ## иҒ”зі»ж–№ејҸ | iDP / IDP API зӮ№ж•°иҙӯд№° | жіЁеҶҢжңәзӣёе…і | | --- | --- | | иҜ·иҒ”зі» @иҜҘйҡҗ | иҜ·иҒ”зі» @жңҙеңЈдҪ‘ | | | | ## еҠҹиғҪ - еҚ•иҙҰеҸ·з”ҹжҲҗгҖҒжҺҲжқғгҖҒжҺЁйҖҒ Sub2APIгҖӮ - жү№йҮҸеӨҡзәҝзЁӢ TUIпјҡ - иҫ“е…ҘиҙҰеҸ·ж•°йҮҸе’ҢзәҝзЁӢж•°гҖӮ - жҜҸдёӘд»»еҠЎеӨұиҙҘжңҖеӨҡйҮҚиҜ• 5 ж¬ЎпјҢеӨұиҙҘеҗҺж Үи®°иҜҘд»»еҠЎеӨұиҙҘе№¶з»§з»ӯеҗҺз»ӯд»»еҠЎгҖӮ - иҝҗиЎҢдёӯжҳҫзӨәжҲҗеҠҹж•°гҖҒеӨұиҙҘж•°гҖҒиҝҗиЎҢдёӯж•°гҖҒзӯүеҫ…ж•°гҖӮ - жңҖз»Ҳиҫ“еҮәз»ҹи®Ўж–Үжң¬пјҢдёҚеңЁз»Ҳз«Ҝжү“еҚ° token JSONгҖӮ - зәҜ HTTP еҚҸи®®жөҒзЁӢпјӣж— жөҸи§ҲеҷЁ fallbackгҖӮ - ж—Ҙеҝ—е’Ң artifact й»ҳи®Өи„ұж•ҸгҖӮ ## е®үиЈ… ```bash cd <йЎ№зӣ®зӣ®еҪ•> python3 -m pip install -e . ``` еҰӮжһңеҸӘзӣҙжҺҘиҝҗиЎҢи„ҡжң¬пјҢзЎ®дҝқеҪ“еүҚ Python е·Іе®үиЈ…пјҡ ```bash python3 -m pip install 'curl_cffi>=0.7' ``` ## й…ҚзҪ® еӨҚеҲ¶зӨәдҫӢй…ҚзҪ®пјҡ ```bash cp .env.example .env ``` еЎ«еҶҷпјҡ ```env IDP_BASE=http://idp.fdvctte.info IDP_TOKEN= SUB2API_URL= SUB2API_EMAIL= SUB2API_PASSWORD= ``` еҸҜйҖүпјҡ ```env IDP_CLIENT_ID= IDP_CHANNEL_ID= IDP_DOMAIN= SUB2API_MODEL_WHITELIST= REQUEST_TIMEOUT=60 ``` ## еҚ•иҙҰеҸ·иҝҗиЎҢ з”ҹжҲҗж–°иҙҰеҸ·е№¶жҺЁйҖҒ Sub2APIпјҡ ```bash python3 scripts/run_idp_codex.py --timeout 60 ``` еӨҚз”Ёе·Іжңү IDP account_idпјҡ ```bash python3 scripts/run_idp_codex.py --account-id 1638 --timeout 60 ``` еҸӘи·‘ OAuthпјҢдёҚжҺЁйҖҒпјҡ ```bash python3 scripts/run_idp_codex.py --timeout 60 --no-sub2api ``` ## жү№йҮҸ TUI дәӨдә’ејҸпјҡ ```bash python3 scripts/run_batch_tui.py ``` йқһдәӨдә’пјҡ ```bash python3 scripts/run_batch_tui.py --count 10 --threads 3 --yes ``` жҢҮе®ҡйҮҚиҜ•ж¬Ўж•°пјҡ ```bash python3 scripts/run_batch_tui.py --count 10 --threads 3 --retries 5 --yes ``` еҸӘи·‘ OAuthпјҢдёҚжҺЁйҖҒпјҡ ```bash python3 scripts/run_batch_tui.py --count 5 --threads 2 --no-sub2api --yes ``` ## иҫ“еҮә еҚ•иҙҰеҸ·иҫ“еҮәзӣ®еҪ•пјҡ ```text artifacts/idp_codex/ ``` жү№йҮҸиҫ“еҮәзӣ®еҪ•пјҡ ```text artifacts/batch_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS/ в”ңв”Җв”Җ summary.json в”ңв”Җв”Җ task_0001/ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ attempt_01/ в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ ... в””в”Җв”Җ task_0002/ в””в”Җв”Җ ... ``` `artifacts/` жҳҜиҝҗиЎҢдә§зү©зӣ®еҪ•пјҢе·Іиў« `.gitignore` еҝҪз•ҘпјӣеҸӘдҝқз•ҷ `artifacts/.gitkeep`гҖӮ жё…зҗҶиҝҗиЎҢдә§зү©пјҡ ```bash find artifacts -mindepth 1 ! -name .gitkeep -delete ``` ## жөӢиҜ• ```bash python3 -m pytest -q ``` ## зӣ®еҪ• ```text lib/ в”ңв”Җв”Җ batch_tui.py # жү№йҮҸеӨҡзәҝзЁӢ TUI в”ңв”Җв”Җ cli.py # еҚ•иҙҰеҸ· CLI зј–жҺ’ в”ңв”Җв”Җ codex_oauth.py # PKCE / OAuth URL / token и§Јжһҗ в”ңв”Җв”Җ config.py # .env е’Ң CLI й…ҚзҪ® в”ңв”Җв”Җ errors.py # йЎ№зӣ®ејӮеёёзұ»еһӢ в”ңв”Җв”Җ idp_client.py # IDP API в”ңв”Җв”Җ logging_utils.py # и„ұж•Ҹ JSONL ж—Ҙеҝ— в”ңв”Җв”Җ sso_http_flow.py # зәҜ HTTP SSO/OAuth дё»жөҒзЁӢ в””в”Җв”Җ sub2api_export.py # Sub2API еҜјеҮә ```

canwhite/MicroRAG.content:
# MicroRAG

A minimal Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system implemented from scratch in pure Python, without any external ML libraries.

**зәҜPythonд»Һйӣ¶е®һзҺ°зҡ„жңҖе°ҸеҸҜиҝҗиЎҢRAGзі»з»ҹпјҢж— еӨ–йғЁжңәеҷЁеӯҰд№ дҫқиө–гҖӮ**

---

## ж ёеҝғзү№жҖ§ / Core Features

| зү№жҖ§ / Feature | иҜҙжҳҺ / Description |
|----------------|-------------------|
| **Retriever** | еҹәдәҺеҜ№жҜ”еӯҰд№ пјҲInfoNCEпјүзҡ„еҗ‘йҮҸжЈҖзҙў / Cosine-similarity retrieval with contrastive learning |
| **Generator** | еҚ•еұӮ GPTпјҢеӯ—з¬Ұзә§ tokenizer / Single-layer GPT with character-level tokenizer |
| **ж— еӨ–йғЁдҫқиө– / No deps** | д»…з”Ё Python ж ҮеҮҶеә“е®һзҺ°иҮӘеҠЁеҫ®еҲҶ / Pure Python autograd with stdlib only |
| **еҸҜиҝҗиЎҢ / Runnable** | и®ӯз»ғ + жҺЁзҗҶе®Ңж•ҙжөҒзЁӢпјҢејҖз®ұеҚіз”Ё / Full train + inference pipeline, ready to run |

---

## жһ¶жһ„ / Architecture

```
з”ЁжҲ·й—®йўҳ вҶ’ Retriever(жЈҖзҙў) вҶ’ жӢјжҺҘдёҠдёӢж–Ү вҶ’ Generator(GPTз”ҹжҲҗ) вҶ’ зӯ”жЎҲ
Query      retrieve        concat context   generate           answer
```

### Retriever

- Query е’Ң Document еҲҶеҲ«йҖҡиҝҮдёҚеҗҢзҡ„еөҢе…Ҙзҹ©йҳөжҳ е°„еҲ° 32 з»ҙеҗ‘йҮҸ
- InfoNCE еҜ№жҜ”еӯҰд№ и®ӯз»ғпјҡжӯЈж ·жң¬зӣёдјјеәҰеә”жҜ”иҙҹж ·жң¬й«ҳ 0.5
- жҺЁзҗҶйҳ¶ж®өдҪҝз”Ё cosine зӣёдјјеәҰжЈҖзҙў

### Generator

- еҚ•еұӮиҮӘжіЁж„ҸеҠӣпјҲcausal maskпјҢж—  sliding windowпјү
- еӯ—з¬Ұзә§ tokenizerпјҲиҜҚиЎЁд»…еҮ еҚҒдёӘ tokenпјү
- RMSNorm + MLP + LM Head
- и®ӯз»ғпјҡnext-token prediction

---

## иҝҗиЎҢ / Run

```bash
uv run main.py
```

иҫ“еҮәзӨәдҫӢпјҡ
```
vocab_size=57, TOTAL_VOCAB=60, EMBED_DIM=32
chars:  ,гҖӮ0-9,...A...Z...a...z...
num params: 23888
step    0 | loss 0.7368 | sp=0.112 sn=0.431
step  200 | loss 0.0000 | sp=0.999 sn=0.000
step  400 | loss 0.0000 | sp=0.999 sn=0.000
step  600 | loss 0.0000 | sp=0.999 sn=0.000
step  800 | loss 0.0000 | sp=1.000 sn=0.000

--- и®ӯз»ғ Generator ---
gpt step    0 | loss 4.4424
gpt step  500 | loss 0.1389
gpt step 1000 | loss 0.0152
gpt step 1500 | loss 0.0005

--- RAG е®Ңж•ҙжөҒзЁӢжөӢиҜ• ---
й—®: еӨӘйҳіжңүеӨҡзғӯпјҹ
зӯ”: еӨӘйҳіиЎЁйқўзәҰ6000еәҰпјҢж ёеҝғзәҰ1500дёҮеәҰгҖӮ
```

---

## и¶…еҸӮж•° / Hyperparameters

| еҸӮж•° / Param | еҖј / Value | еҗ«д№ү / Meaning |
|-------------|------------|----------------|
| `EMBED_DIM` | 32 | еөҢе…Ҙз»ҙеәҰ / Embedding dimension |
| `BLOCK_SIZE` | 64 | дҪҚзҪ®зј–з ҒиЎЁеӨ§е°ҸпјҲдҪҚзҪ® > 64 еӨҚз”Ё wpe[63]пјү|
| `lr` (Retriever) | 0.01 | жЈҖзҙўеҷЁеӯҰд№ зҺҮ |
| `gpt_lr` | 0.05 | з”ҹжҲҗеҷЁеӯҰд№ зҺҮ |
| `retriever_steps` | 10 | жЈҖзҙўеҷЁи®ӯз»ғжӯҘж•° |
| `gpt_steps` | 2000 | з”ҹжҲҗеҷЁи®ӯз»ғжӯҘж•° |

---

tastyeffectco/sandboxd.content:
sandboxd

The open-source engine for AI app-builder products.
Give every user an isolated cloud dev environment, a built-in coding agent, and a live preview URL вҖ” self-hosted, on one machine, in one command.

--- ## What is sandboxd? (start here) Think of the apps where you type *"build me a todo app"* and seconds later a working website appears at its own link вҖ” like Lovable, Bolt, v0, or Replit. **sandboxd is the open-source backend that makes that possible**, running on your own server. Here's what it does, in plain terms. You send it one HTTP request, and it: 1. **Creates a sandbox** вҖ” a private, isolated Linux container (its own filesystem, its own memory limits), so one user's code can never see or break another's. 2. **Runs an AI coding agent inside it** вҖ” you give it a prompt, and it writes the code into that sandbox. (The OpenCode and Claude Code CLIs come pre-installed.) 3. **Gives the app a live URL** вҖ” the dev server running inside the sandbox is instantly reachable at a shareable preview link. ``` POST /sandbox вҶ’ a private, isolated container spins up POST .../tasks вҶ’ an AI agent writes an app inside it http://.preview... вҶ’ that app is live at its own URL ``` It's also cheap to run: a sandbox **goes to sleep when nobody's using it** (freeing memory) and **wakes up the instant someone opens its link again** вҖ” files are saved on disk the whole time. So one ordinary server can hold many users instead of needing one virtual machine each. Under the hood it's deliberately small and easy to understand: **one Go program that tells Docker what to do**, with **Traefik** handling the URLs and **SQLite** as the database. No Kubernetes, no separate database server, no message queue вҖ” you could read the whole thing in an afternoon. ``` в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ your host (just needs Docker) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ browser в”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ Traefik в”Җв”Җв–¶ sandbox (coding agent + dev server :3000) в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в–І в–І в–І в”Ӯ API/CLI в”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ sandboxd в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җ workspace dir (persists) в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ SQLite (source of truth) В· idleвҶ’stop В· requestвҶ’wake в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ-в”Җв”ҳ ``` ### Who's it for? **вң… Use it if** you're running **many sandboxes foвҖҰ

amElnagdy/guard-skills.content:
# guard-skills

[![skills.sh](https://skills.sh/b/amElnagdy/guard-skills)](https://skills.sh/amElnagdy/guard-skills)

Focused **guard skills** for coding agents: second-pass quality gates that catch the systematic failure modes of AI-generated code, tests, and docs before they ship.

Best use: let your agent do the work, then invoke the relevant guard on the diff before you present, commit, or merge it. These skills can guide writing when you explicitly ask for that, but they are strongest as reactive review passes.

## Install

Browse the package first:

```bash
npx skills add amElnagdy/guard-skills --list
```

Install the package:

```bash
npx skills add amElnagdy/guard-skills
```

Or install one guard:

```bash
npx skills add amElnagdy/guard-skills --skill clean-code-guard
npx skills add amElnagdy/guard-skills --skill test-guard
npx skills add amElnagdy/guard-skills --skill docs-guard
npx skills add amElnagdy/guard-skills --skill wp-guard
npx skills add amElnagdy/guard-skills --skill woo-guard
```

Install for a specific agent:

```bash
npx skills add amElnagdy/guard-skills --skill clean-code-guard --agent codex
npx skills add amElnagdy/guard-skills --skill test-guard --agent claude-code
npx skills add amElnagdy/guard-skills --skill '*' --agent cursor
```

Install globally:

```bash
npx skills add amElnagdy/guard-skills --global
```

Works with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, and other supported agents via the [Skills CLI](https://github.com/vercel-labs/skills).

## How to use them

Run a guard after your agent produces work:

```text
Use $clean-code-guard on the diff you just produced.
Use $test-guard on the tests you just wrote.
Use $docs-guard on this README update before we ship it.
Use $wp-guard on this WordPress plugin change.
Use $woo-guard on this WooCommerce checkout change.
```

Use a guard up front only when you want that constraint active while writing:

```text
Use $wp-guard while implementing this REST endpoint, then self-check before delivery.
```

## Which guard to run

| Guard | Use after the agent changed | Catches | Pair with |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `clean-code-guard` | Production code in any language | LLM code smells, over-abstraction, broad error swallowing, bad names, SOLID/DRY/KISS/YAGNI violations | Platform-specific guards |
| `test-guard` | Test code | Mock abuse, duplicate tests, implementation-detail assertions, tests that catch nothing | `clean-code-guard` when test helpers contain real logic |
| `docs-guard` | READMEs, docstrings, API docs, changelogs, tutorials | Hallucinated symbols, broken samples, docs-vs-code drift, unverifiable claims | Any guard whose behavior is documented |
| `wp-guard` | WordPress plugin, theme, block, REST, AJAX, shortcode, query, or WP-CLI code | Escaping, sanitization, nonces, capabilities, prepared queries, i18n, query/caching mistakes | `clean-code-guard`; `woo-guard` when Woo APIs appear |
| `woo-guard` | WooCommerce extension, checkout, order, product, gateway, shippiвҖҰ

mysk-research/loupe.content:
# Loupe Loupe is an iOS and iPadOS app that gives you a hands-on tour of the device fingerprinting surface. It reads real values from public iOS APIs, the same ones any third-party app can call, and shows them to you raw. The point is simple: see what your iPhone quietly exposes, and why each reading helps an app recognize you again. Trackers don't need your name, email, or location to recognize you online. Each reading isn't necessarily unique on its own, but together they form a fingerprint that follows you across apps and websites.

## How signals are organized Loupe groups every reading into three tiers, reflecting the cost of access: - **Passive** вҖ” visible to any app with no prompt at all (locale, time zone, screen, battery, and more). - **Needs Permission** вҖ” readings that trigger an iOS prompt (contacts, photos, location, calendars). - **Advanced** вҖ” clever side-channel uses of public APIs, such as URL-scheme probing via `canOpenURL` and Keychain persistence across reinstalls. ## Privacy Nothing Loupe reads leaves your device unless you explicitly export it. Values are shown raw, without aggregation or hashing. Nothing is uploaded, synced, or shared. ## A note on how this was built Loupe was written almost entirely by AI coding tools. ## Building You'll need Xcode 26 or newer. 1. Open `code/Loupe.xcodeproj`. 2. Copy `code/Config/Signing.local.xcconfig.example` to `code/Config/Signing.local.xcconfig` and fill in your own `DEVELOPMENT_TEAM` and bundle identifiers. This file is gitignored and never published. 3. Build and run on a device or simulator. The project uses Xcode's buildable folders (folder references), so new Swift files are picked up automatically with no need to edit the project file. ### macOS Loupe also builds for macOS. The Mac version is mostly complete, but a few things still need work before it's polished. ## Support the project Loupe is free and open source. If it helped you see what apps can quietly learn about your device, the best way to support more work like this is to try [Psylo](https://apps.apple.com/app/psylo-private-browser-proxy/id6741358035), our privacy-first browser for iPhone and iPad. Psylo gives you proxy-backed browsing, isolated tabs, and anti-fingerprinting protections. You can also read [why we built Psylo](https://mysk.blog/2025/06/17/introducing-psylo/).вҖҰ

YuvBindal/produck-oss.content:
# рҹҰҶ Produck

**Your users talk. Your fixes ship.**

Produck turns user feedback into shipped code. Users report issues right where they happen вҖ” in your app, on the exact element that's broken вҖ” and Produck's coding agents pick it up, reproduce it, write the fix, and open the PR. All you do is review and merge.

## How it works

1. **рҹҺҘ Capture** вҖ” Users click the Produck widget embedded in your app to record their screen, draw over live pages, and speak or submit bugs, issues, and feature requests in context. Every report is pinned to the exact element with a page snapshot.
2. **рҹӨ– Auto-fix** вҖ” Produck's coding agents triage the session replays, reproduce the issue, implement and test the fix, then open a PR.
3. **рҹ”” Close the loop** вҖ” When the fix ships, the user who reported it gets pinged: *"fixed."* Complainers become loyal co-builders.

## Two ways to collect feedback

- **Embeddable SDK** вҖ” drop one script tag into your product so users report issues where they happen.
- **Chrome extension** вҖ” capture feedback on any site.

## Why

AI made building apps easy вҖ” the hard part now is knowing **what** to build and shipping the fix before users churn. Feedback tooling is stuck in 2015: surveys, NPS, session replays. None of them ship code. Produck is the feedback loop that closes itself: a user's voice, pinned to a pixel, shipped as code, returned to them as a notification.

## Links

- рҹҢҗ Website: [tryproduck.com](https://tryproduck.com)
- рҹ“ҡ Docs: [docs.tryproduck.com](https://docs.tryproduck.com)

Parcle-AI/parcle-memory.content:
# Parcle **Long-term memory for AI agents** Ingest conversations and files, then ask questions in natural language and get cited answers back. Give every user a private, persistent agent memory.
--- ## Why Parcle? LLMs forget everything between calls. Parcle gives every user a private memory you can write to and search: - рҹ§  **Per-user memory** вҖ” scope everything to a `user_id`. - рҹ’¬ **Ingest anything** вҖ” chat transcripts and files (PDF, Markdown, text, вҖҰ) go in the same place. - рҹ”Һ **Ask, don't query** вҖ” search returns a synthesized **answer** with **citations**, not just raw chunks. ## Installation ```bash pip install parcle ``` ## Quickstart ```python from parcle import Parcle # Reads PARCLE_API_KEY from the environment if api_key is omitted. client = Parcle(api_key="pk_live_...") # 1. Write a conversation into a user's memory. client.ingest_dialog( user_id="ada", messages=[ {"role": "user", "content": "I'm allergic to peanuts."}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "Got it вҖ” I'll avoid peanuts in suggestions."}, ], ) # 2. ...or ingest a file (PDF, Markdown, text, вҖҰ). client.ingest_file(user_id="ada", file="diet-notes.pdf") # 3. Ask a question. You get an answer with confidence and citations. result = client.search(user_id="ada", query="What food should I avoid?") print(result.answer) # "You're allergic to peanuts, so avoid them." print(result.confidence) # 0.92 print(result.citations) # [Citation(type="dialog", id="...")] ```

diffusionstudio/lottie.content:
# Text-To-Lottie

This project includes a full-screen Lottie player built on **Skia CanvasKit (Skottie)**, with a
React + shadcn/ui + TypeScript control surface. Built to **generate a Lottie
animation with an LLM and watch it play live**: the agent writes
`public/lottie.json`, the dev server hot-reloads it.

## Usage 

Run
```bash
npx skills add diffusionstudio/lottie
```

Then ask claude code/codex or any other agent supporting skills to generate an animation using `text-to-lottie`

## Getting started

Alternatively you can set up the repository manually

```bash
npm install   # also copies the CanvasKit wasm into /public (postinstall)
npm run dev
```

Then open the printed local URL.

## CanvasKit wasm

The wasm binary is **not** committed; it is copied from
`node_modules/canvaskit-wasm/bin/full/canvaskit.wasm` into `public/` by
[`scripts/copy-canvaskit.mjs`](scripts/copy-canvaskit.mjs) on `postinstall`. Run
it manually any time with `node scripts/copy-canvaskit.mjs`.

GordenSun/GordenSuperPPTSkills.content:
# Gorden Super PPT SkillsпјҲжҠҖиғҪеҢ…пјү

AI PPTиөӣйҒ“з»Ҳз»“иҖ…пјҢеҸІдёҠжңҖжңҖжңҖејә PPT SkillпјҒпјҒпјҒ

дҪҝз”ЁGPTз”ҹжҲҗиұӘеҚҺзҡ„еӣҫзүҮж јејҸPPTпјҢз„¶еҗҺиҪ¬жҚўдёә**е®Ңе…ЁеҸҜзј–иҫ‘**зҡ„PPTXж–Үд»¶гҖӮ

Skillе…ЁжөҒзЁӢе·ІжӢҶжҲҗ **3 дёӘзӢ¬з«ӢжҠҖиғҪ**пјҢеҸҜд»ҘжӢҶеҲҶдҪҝз”Ёе’ҢдјҳеҢ–пјҡ

| жҠҖиғҪ | дҪңз”Ё | иҫ“е…Ҙ вҶ’ иҫ“еҮә |
|---|---|---|
| **[GordenImagePPTGen](GordenImagePPTGen/SKILL.md)** | з”ҹжҲҗгҖҢеӣҫзүҮж јејҸзҡ„ PPTгҖҚ | дё»йўҳ/еҶ…е®№ вҶ’ жҜҸйЎө .png + еӣҫзүҮеһӢ .pptx |
| **[GordenImage2PPTX](GordenImage2PPTX/SKILL.md)** | жҠҠгҖҢеӣҫзүҮ PPT / еӣҫзүҮгҖҚиҝҳеҺҹжҲҗеҸҜзј–иҫ‘ pptx | еӣҫзүҮ вҶ’ еҸҜзј–иҫ‘ .pptxпјҲиғҢжҷҜ+йӘЁжһ¶+еӣҫж Ү+ж–Үжң¬ еӣӣеұӮпјү |
| **[GordenSuperPPTSkill](GordenSuperPPTSkill/SKILL.md)** | жү“еҢ…зј–жҺ’еүҚдёӨиҖ…пјҢдҫқж¬Ўжү§иЎҢ | дё»йўҳ/еҶ…е®№ вҶ’ еӣҫзүҮеһӢ PPT + еҸҜзј–иҫ‘ pptx |

- еҸӘиҰҒеҒҡеӣҫзүҮзүҲ PPT вҶ’ **GordenImagePPTGen**
- еҸӘжҠҠеӣҫзүҮиҪ¬еҸҜзј–иҫ‘ вҶ’ **GordenImage2PPTX**
- дёҖй”®"е…ҲеҮәеӣҫеҶҚиҪ¬еҸҜзј–иҫ‘" / жңӘжҢҮе®ҡ вҶ’ **GordenSuperPPTSkill**пјҲAвҶ’B дёІиҒ”пјү

## ж•Ҳжһңеұ•зӨә
1гҖҒGordenImagePPTGenпјҲGordenзҡ„еӣҫзүҮPPTз”ҹжҲҗжҠҖиғҪпјүз”ҹжҲҗеӣҫзүҮж јејҸзҡ„PPT

![GordenImagePPTGen з”ҹжҲҗзҡ„еӣҫзүҮж јејҸ PPT зӨәдҫӢ](examples/example-Image-ppt.png)

2гҖҒGordenImage2PPTXпјҲGordenзҡ„еӣҫзүҮиҪ¬PPTXжҠҖиғҪпјүжҠҠеӣҫзүҮиҪ¬жҚўдёәе®Ңе…ЁеҸҜзј–иҫ‘зҡ„PPTXж–Үд»¶

![GordenImage2PPTX иҪ¬жҚўеҗҺзҡ„еҸҜзј–иҫ‘ PPTX зӨәдҫӢ](examples/example-editable-pptx.png)

## еҰӮдҪ•дҪҝз”Ё
д»…йҷҗCodexдҪҝз”ЁгҖӮ

з¬¬1жӯҘпјҡжҠҠGithubд»“еә“ең°еқҖеҸ‘з»ҷCodexи®©д»–е®үиЈ…жҠҖиғҪпјӣ

з¬¬2жӯҘпјҡжҢүйңҖдҪҝз”ЁгҖӮGPT 5.5жЁЎеһӢпјҢжҺЁзҗҶејәеәҰйҖү"дёӯ"еҚіеҸҜгҖӮ
еҰӮжһңеҸӘз”ҹжҲҗеӣҫзүҮж јејҸPPTпјҢжҸҗзӨәиҜҚпјҡ
```
дҪҝз”ЁGordenImagePPTGenжҠҖиғҪпјҢз”ҹжҲҗдёҖдёӘNйЎөзҡ„PPTпјҢеҶ…е®№дёәXXXпјҢиҰҒжұӮPPTиҰҒжұӮиұӘеҚҺгҖҒдҝЎжҒҜеҜҶеәҰй«ҳгҖҒжҺ’зүҲеӨҚжқӮ
```

еҰӮжһңеҸӘжғі**жҠҠеӣҫзүҮPPTиҪ¬жҚўжҲҗеҸҜзј–иҫ‘зҡ„PPTXж–Үд»¶**пјҢжҸҗзӨәиҜҚпјҡ
```

```

иҜҙжҳҺпјҡ
жң¬жҠҖиғҪд»…йҖӮз”ЁдәҺCodexпјҢеӣ дёәеҝ…йЎ»дҪҝз”ЁGPTз”ҹжҲҗеӣҫзүҮе’ҢGPTзҡ„и§Ҷи§үиғҪеҠӣпјҢзҗҶи®әдёҠOpus+GPTз”ҹеӣҫжҺҘеҸЈд№ҹеҸҜд»Ҙе®һзҺ°пјҢдҪҶжҳҜжң¬жҠҖиғҪжІЎжңүеҒҡдё“й—Ёзҡ„йҖӮй…ҚгҖӮ

еӣҫзүҮиҪ¬еҸҜзј–иҫ‘PPTXж–Үд»¶пјҢжҜ”иҫғиҙ№йўқеәҰпјҢиҪ¬жҚў1еј еӣҫзүҮеӨ§жҰӮиҖ—иҙ№Plusи®ўйҳ…5е°Ҹж—¶йўқеәҰзҡ„10%гҖӮ

жЎҶжһ¶еӣҫй»ҳи®ӨжҳҜж•ҙдҪ“зҡ„дёҖеј еӣҫпјҢд№ҹж”ҜжҢҒжӢҶеҲҶжҲҗдёҖдёӘдёӘзӢ¬з«Ӣзҡ„жЎҶжһ¶жЁЎеқ—еӣҫпјҢжҸҗзӨәиҜҚйҮҢжҳҺзЎ®е‘ҠиҜүCodexеҚіеҸҜгҖӮ

## еҺҹзҗҶи®Іи§Ј
ж ёеҝғдҪҝз”Ёзҡ„жҳҜGPTзҡ„з”ҹеӣҫиғҪеҠӣе’Ңи§Ҷи§үи§ЈжһҗиғҪеҠӣгҖӮ
еӨ§иҮҙжӯҘйӘӨжҳҜпјҡдҫқж¬ЎжҸҗеҸ–PPTеӣҫзүҮзҡ„иғҢжҷҜеӣҫгҖҒжЎҶжһ¶еӣҫгҖҒеӣҫж Үе’ҢиЈ…йҘ°еӣҫгҖҒж–Үжң¬гҖӮжңҖеҗҺеңЁPPTйҮҢжҢүеқҗж ҮжӢјиЈ…иө·жқҘгҖӮеҪ“з„¶дёәдәҶе®һзҺ°е®ҢзҫҺзҡ„ж•ҲжһңпјҢеҒҡдәҶеҫҲеӨҡз»ҶиҠӮйӘҢиҜҒе’ҢзәҰжқҹи§„еҲҷгҖӮ
дҪҝз”ЁиҝҮзЁӢдёӯпјҢдҪ иғҪзңӢеҲ°GPTз”ҹжҲҗзҡ„иҝҮзЁӢеӣҫзүҮгҖӮ

## е®үиЈ…пјҲз»ҷAIзңӢзҡ„пјү

```bash
# CodexпјҲжҢүйңҖйҖүиЈ…е…¶дёҖ/е…ЁйғЁпјү
cp -R GordenImagePPTGen   "${CODEX_HOME:-$HOME/.codex}/skills/GordenImagePPTGen"

pip3 install python-pptx pillow numpy

```
GordenSuperPPTSkills/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ README.md                  вҶҗ жң¬ж–Үд»¶пјҲжҖ»е…ҘеҸЈпјү

```

жҜҸдёӘжҠҖиғҪзӣ®еҪ•еқҮ**иҮӘеҢ…еҗ«**гҖӮжҠҠж•ҙдёӘ `GordenPPTSuperSkills/` еӨҚеҲ¶еҲ°зӣ®ж Ү Agent зҡ„ skills зӣ®еҪ•еҚіеҸҜдҪҝз”ЁгҖӮ

IvanSobolev/Neo3dEngine.content: # рҹ’» Neo 3D Console (C#) A minimalist 3D engine written in C# that runs entirely inside the system console. This project was built from scratch without any external graphical libraries or modern graphics APIs (like OpenGL or DirectX). EverythingвҖ”from custom vector mathematics to ray casting/tracing and the TCP network stackвҖ”is computed entirely on the CPU. This project was designed for educational and demonstration purposes for a [YouTube video](https://youtu.be/vhYE882B9dE). --- ## рҹҡҖ Key Features 1. **CPU Raycasting / Raytracing**: * Renders polygonal meshes (`.obj` format) and geometric spheres. * Intersection optimization using Bounding Sphere checks before performing detailed triangle intersection calculations. 2. **Dynamic Lighting and Shadows**: * Light intensity attenuation based on distance. * Lambertian diffuse shading using surface normals. * Real-time shadow rendering via Shadow Rays cast from intersection points back to light sources. 3. **Multi-threaded Rendering**: * Leverages `Parallel.For` to distribute pixel rendering calculations across all available CPU cores. 4. **Optimized Console Output**: * Map light intensity to a character gradient string (`" .:!/r(l1Z4H9W8$@"`) to simulate shading. * Frame buffering with grouped-color output to minimize slow, native OS terminal print API calls. 5. **Custom 3D Mathematics**: * Custom implementations of `Vector3`, `Vector2`, `Ray`, and rotation matrices (Euler rotations around X, Y, and Z axes). * Manual implementation of the MГ¶llerвҖ“Trumbore ray-triangle intersection algorithm. 6. **Low-level Window Input**: * Asynchronous, non-blocking keyboard polling using the Win32 API (`GetAsyncKeyState`), ensuring key presses are detected only when the console window is active. 7. **Custom Network Multiplayer**: * Client-server TCP network manager built from scratch. * Custom binary packet serialization, routing via stable hash-based type IDs, and a decoupled event subscription model. * Included multiplayer demo featuring real-time player position synchronization and a text-based chat lobby. --- ## рҹҸ—пёҸ Architecture Overview The engine is decoupled into clear logical layers: ``` в”ңв”Җв”Җ AbstractClass # Base classes for GameObjects, Scenes, Screens, and Lights в”ңв”Җв”Җ Implementation # Concrete cameras, rendering managers, and screen renderers в”ңв”Җв”Җ Interfaces # Contracts for loose coupling (ICamera, IScreen, etc.) в”ңв”Җв”Җ Shape # Geometric primitives (Sphere, Triangle, Object3D) в”ңв”Җв”Җ StaticClass # Helper utilities (ObjLoader, GameTime tracking) в”ңв”Җв”Җ Structure # Core structs (Vectors, Rays, RenderData payload) в”ңв”Җв”Җ UI # Overlay system for rendering text on top of the frame buffer в””в”Җв”Җ Network # NetвҖҰ

JFGAtlas/aethergate-pro.content:
# AetherGate Pro рҹҢҢ

AetherGate Pro is an enterprise-grade, high-availability VPN & Proxy Gateway manager designed to run on Linux Virtual Private Servers (VPS). Built by and for proxy network engineers, it solves the notorious issue of SSH disconnection and routing conflicts commonly experienced when running VPN gateways directly on VPS host interfaces.

By isolating all VPN activity inside a dedicated Linux **Network Namespace (`netns`)** and routing proxy traffic via an asynchronous single-port mixed protocol (SOCKS5/HTTP) server, AetherGate Pro provides a seamless, self-healing proxy gateway with a gorgeous glassmorphic dashboard.

---

## рҹҡҖ Key Features

* **Network Namespace Isolation (`netns`)**: Restricted default routing within `vpn_ns` namespace prevents host interface pollution. Say goodbye to SSH drops and disconnected VPS sessions!
* **Mixed Protocol Single-Port Proxy**: Auto-sniffs incoming client connections on port `7928` to serve both **SOCKS5** and **HTTP** protocols on a single port.
* **Auto-Geolocation & Scamalytics Fraud Score Filtering**: Concurrently scrapes VPNGate, performs latency pinging, runs IP geolocations, and checks Scamalytics fraud risks. Hides nodes with a fraud score $\ge 10$ to guarantee pure residential/mobile outbound traffic.
* **Modern Web Dashboard**: A futuristic, glassmorphic dark-themed control center featuring mouse spotlight tracking, real-time diagnostic consoles, auto-refreshing connection tables, and lock settings.
* **Watchdog Self-Healing**: Automatically monitors the VPN tunnel. If a node fails or latency spikes, the background watchdog triggers auto-reconnection/failover.
* **Systemd Daemon Integration**: Designed to run as a persistent system service that auto-starts on system boot.

---

## рҹӣ пёҸ Architecture Overview

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph Host OS [Host OS Network Space]
        SSH[SSH Session - Port 22] -->|No interference| HostRouting[Host Routing Table]
        User[Admin Browser] -->|Port 8787| WebUI[Async Dashboard Server]
        ClientProxy[Client Proxy App] -->|Port 7928| SocatForwarder[socat Port Forwarder]
    end

    subgraph NetNS [vpn_ns isolated namespace]
        SocatForwarder -->|Forward tunnel| NamespaceProxy[Mixed SOCKS5/HTTP Proxy]
        NamespaceProxy -->|Outbound request| OpenVPN[OpenVPN Client Process]
        OpenVPN -->|Tunnel Interface tun0| VirtualEthernet[veth_vpn: 10.200.0.2]
    end

    VirtualEthernet <-->|NAT Masquerade| veth_host[veth_host: 10.200.0.1]
    veth_host <--> Internet((Global Internet))
    OpenVPN <-->|Encrypted Tunnel| VPNGateNode[VPNGate Target Node]
```

---

## рҹ“Ҙ VPS Deployment Guide

This guide describes how to deploy **AetherGate Pro** on a clean Linux VPS (Ubuntu 20.04/22.04+ or Debian 11+ recommended).

### 1. Prerequisites
Ensure you have root access to your VPS. Connect via SSH:
```bash
ssh root@your_vps_ip
```

### 2. Copy Codebase to VPS
You can clone this repository to your VPS directory (e.g., `/tmp/aethergate-вҖҰ

> жҳҜе…ЁзЁӢд»Ҙи®ҫе®ҡиҜӯиЁҖдёҺдҪ дәӨдә’пјҢе№¶жҗңзҙўзӣ®ж ҮиҜӯиЁҖзҡ„иө„ж–ҷпјҢдёҚжҳҜз®ҖеҚ•зҡ„зҝ»иҜ‘иҫ“еҮәгҖӮ

---

## дёҖгҖҒдёәд»Җд№ҲдҪ йңҖиҰҒиҝҷдёӘ

и®© AI её®дҪ еҒҡи°ғз ”пјҢдҪ еӨ§жҰӮзҺҮзў°иҝҮиҝҷдәӣеқ‘пјҡ

жҗңзҙў + жҖ»з»“ вҶ’ еӨӘжө…пјҢеҮәжқҘеҮ жқЎж‘ҳиҰҒпјҢжІЎжңүзәөж·ұгҖӮ
иЎҢдёҡжҠҘе‘ҠжҢүд»Ҫж”¶иҙ№ $50вҖ“500+ вҶ’ еӨӘиҙөпјҢдёӘдәәз”ЁдёҚиө·гҖӮ
жө·еӨ–е·Ҙе…· вҶ’ жҗңдёҚеҲ°еӣҪеҶ…иө„жәҗеҰӮпјҡзҷҫеәҰзҷҫз§‘гҖҒзҹҘд№ҺгҖҒ199ITгҖҒиүҫз‘һгҖӮ
AI зј–ж•°еӯ— вҶ’ зңӢиө·жқҘеҗҲзҗҶпјҢдҪҶжүҫдёҚеҲ°жқҘжәҗгҖӮ

иҝҷдёӘ skill иө°е®Ң **4 еұӮжөҒзЁӢ**жүҚдәӨжҠҘе‘ҠгҖӮдёҚжҳҜжҗңе®Ңе°ұеҮәпјҢжҳҜжһҗвҶ’жҗңйӘҢвҶ’еҶҷвҶ’йӘҢгҖӮ

## дәҢгҖҒи°ҒйҖӮеҗҲз”Ё

**зӢ¬з«ӢејҖеҸ‘иҖ…**гҖҒ**зӢ¬з«Ӣз ”з©¶иҖ…**гҖҒ**е°Ҹеӣўйҳҹ**гҖӮ
йңҖиҰҒдё“дёҡзә§и°ғз ”иғҪеҠӣпјҢдҪҶдёҚжғідҫқиө–д»ҳиҙ№ж•°жҚ®еә“жҲ–з ”з©¶жңәжһ„зҡ„дәәгҖӮ

## дёүгҖҒдёҖж¬Ўж ҮеҮҶжЁЎејҸи°ғз ”зҡ„иҫ“еҮә


| жҢҮж Ү      | ж•°жҚ®пјҲstandard жЁЎејҸзӨәдҫӢпјү                           |
| ------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| жҠҘе‘Ҡй•ҝеәҰ    | 500-700 иЎҢ / зәҰ 12,000-18,000 еӯ—               |
| ж•°жҚ®иЎЁ     | 15-25 еј пјҢиҰҶзӣ–еёӮеңәи§„жЁЎгҖҒз«һдәүж јеұҖгҖҒжҠҖжңҜеҸӮж•°зӯүеӨҡдёӘз»ҙеәҰ               |
| еҲҶжһҗж®өиҗҪ    | 80-120 ж®өпјҲжҜҸж®өеҗ«з»“и®ә + ж•°жҚ® + еӣ жһң + еҲӨж–ӯпјү              |
| еј•з”Ёзҡ„зӢ¬з«Ӣжңәжһ„ | 15-25 е®¶пјҲдёӯеӣҪдҝЎйҖҡйҷўгҖҒиүҫз‘һе’ЁиҜўгҖҒеӣҪе®¶з»ҹи®ЎеұҖгҖҒзҷҫеәҰзҷҫз§‘гҖҒзҹҘд№ҺгҖҒ36ж°ӘгҖҒжҫҺж№ғж–°й—»зӯүпјү |
| еҸҚж–№и§ӮзӮ№    | 3-8 еӨ„пјҢжҜҸз« иҮіе°‘е‘ҲзҺ°дёҖдёӘдәүи®®жҲ–еҸҚеҜ№и§’еәҰ                       |
| ж•°жҚ®ж”¶йӣҶ    | ~1-3 еҲҶй’ҹ                                     |
| жҠҘе‘Ҡз”ҹжҲҗ    | ~8-15 еҲҶй’ҹ                                    |
| жҖ»иҖ—ж—¶     | ~10-20 еҲҶй’ҹ                                   |


> д»ҘдёҠдёә standard жЁЎејҸе…ёеһӢиҢғеӣҙпјҢе®һйҷ…еӣ дё»йўҳеӨҚжқӮеәҰгҖҒж•°жҚ®еҸҜиҺ·еҸ–жҖ§гҖҒжҗңзҙўеј•ж“Һе“Қеә”зӯүеӣ зҙ жңүжүҖжө®еҠЁгҖӮ|

рҹ“Ӯ **[жҹҘзңӢе…ЁйғЁз”ҹжҲҗжҠҘе‘Ҡ вҶ’](reports/)** зӮ№жӯӨжөҸи§ҲжүҖжңүе·Із”ҹжҲҗзҡ„и°ғз ”жҠҘе‘ҠпјҢеҸҜзӣҙжҺҘзӮ№еҮ»жү“ејҖйҳ…иҜ»гҖӮ

## еӣӣгҖҒе·ҘдҪңйҖ»иҫ‘

ж•ҙдёӘжөҒзЁӢеҲҶ 4 дёӘйҳ¶ж®өпјҢжҢүйЎәеәҸиҮӘеҠЁжү§иЎҢпјҡ

```
в‘  еҲҶжһҗеӨ§зәІ вҖ” еҲҶжһҗдё»йўҳпјҢз”ҹжҲҗи°ғз ”жЎҶжһ¶е’Ңжҗңзҙўи®ЎеҲ’
         вҶ“
в‘Ў йҮҮйӣҶж•°жҚ® вҖ” в•ӯв”Җ еңЁзәҝжЁЎејҸпјҡSearXNG / Exa зә§иҒ”жҗңзҙў вҶ’ Scrapling жү№йҮҸжҠ“еҸ– вҶ’ ж•°жҚ®жұ 
              в•°в”Җ зҰ»зәҝжЁЎејҸпјҡзӣҙжҺҘиҜ»еҸ–жң¬ең°ж–Үд»¶пјҲPDF/DOCX/TXT/MDпјүвҶ’ ж•°жҚ®жұ 
         вҶ“
в‘ў е№¶иЎҢж’°еҶҷ вҖ” еӨҡз« иҠӮеҗҢж—¶ж’°еҶҷпјҢдәӢе®һзӣҙжҺҘеөҢе…Ҙ promptпјҢдёҚеҒҡе·Ҙе…·и°ғз”Ё
         вҶ“
в‘Ј йӘҢж”¶иЈ…й…Қ вҖ” жү№йҮҸ validate вҶ’ assemble-report вҶ’ convert-citations вҶ’ qa-report
```

> дёӨз§ҚжЁЎејҸе…ұдә«з¬¬ в‘ўв‘Ј йҳ¶ж®өгҖӮж•°жҚ®жұ ж јејҸе®Ңе…ЁдёҖиҮҙпјҢз« иҠӮж’°еҶҷе’ҢиЈ…й…ҚиҙЁжЈҖйӣ¶ж”№еҠЁгҖӮ

## дә”гҖҒжҗңзҙўй“ҫи·ҜдёҺеҶ…зҪ®иө„жәҗ

жүҖжңүе·Ҙе…·е·ІеҶ…зҪ®пјҢж— йңҖйўқеӨ–иҙӯд№°гҖӮзі»з»ҹйҮҮз”Ё **дёүеұӮзә§иҒ”жҗңзҙў** зӯ–з•ҘпјҡSearXNGпјҲдҪңиҖ…е·ІйғЁзҪІзҡ„е…ғжҗңзҙўеј•ж“ҺпјҢ70+ еј•ж“Һеҗ«зҷҫеәҰ/Google/BraveпјүвҶ’ ExaпјҲOMO еҶ…зҪ®еҶ·еӨҮпјүвҶ’ еҚҒеӨҡдёӘе…Қиҙ№жҗңзҙўеј•ж“Һ + еӣҪеҶ…ж•°жҚ®жәҗпјҲжңҖз»Ҳе…ңеә•пјүгҖӮжҜҸеұӮеј•ж“ҺжЈҖжөӢйҖҡиҝҮеҚіз”ЁпјҢдёҚз»§з»ӯжҺўжөӢдёӢзә§пјҢзЎ®дҝқе“Қеә”йҖҹеәҰгҖӮж•ҙдёӘжөҒзЁӢеҰӮдёӢпјҡ

```
Layer 1 вҖ” SearXNGпјҲдҪңиҖ…йғЁзҪІпјҢ70+ еј•ж“Һеҗ«зҷҫеәҰ/Google/BraveпјҢејҖз®ұеҚіз”Ёпјү
  вҶ“ дёҚеҸҜз”Ёж—¶

wizenheimer/canary.content:
Canary

QA harness built for Claude Code.

Canary is a QA harness purpose built for coding agents like Claude Code. It reads your code diffs, identifies the affected UI flows, and tests them in real browser instances using Claude Code. Under the hood, it ships with a QuickJS WASM sandbox exposing the full Playwright API, letting Claude automate any long-running UI task вҖ” from handling logins to navigating complicated UIs. Instead of clicking through flows by hand to reproduce and verify issues, Canary provides full session recordings. You get screen recordings with console logs, network requests, HARs, and Playwright traces so you can inspect exactly what the agent did. Every Canary run captures a reusable Playwright script. Letting you re-run it in CI with zero inference cost on replay. Most testing tools force you to choose between two extremes: - An opaque agent run you can't reproduce. - Raw Playwright scripts you have to write and maintain by hand. Canary doesn't make you choose: the agent does the QA and hands you a reproducible script. ## Features - **See exactly what happened.** Trace, video, network, console, and a screenshot of every step вҖ” captured automatically. - **Reproducible by default.** Canary turns each run into a real Playwright script. Let your agent discover a flow once; re-run it forever. - **One file, zero setup.** Every session is a self-contained `report.html` вҖ” open it, commit it, send it. No server, no build. - **Built for agents.** Drop-in plugins for Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex. - **Sandboxed.** Scripts run in a QuickJS WASM sandbox with the full Playwright `Page` API вҖ” no Node, no host access. ## Who it's for https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8459994a-b43c-4483-bb4a-00522d1d03fe You describe the flow in plain language; your agent drives a real browser and hands back **both** a report you can just read **and** the exact Playwright script вҖ” plus the full trace вҖ” behind it. Most tools make you pick one: an opaque agent run you can't reproduce, or raw Playwright you write and maintain by hand. Canary gives you both. | You are aвҖҰ | Instead ofвҖҰ | Canary gives youвҖҰ | | ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Developer** | Writing and maintaining Playwright/E2E scripts by hand | A reusable script captured from every run вҖ” re-run it in CI, no agent cost on replay | | **QA engineer** | Clicking through flows manually to вҖҰ

lbq110/weread-exporter.content:
# weread-exporter

еҫ®дҝЎиҜ»д№Ұе…Ёжң¬еҜјеҮәе·Ҙе…· вҖ” йҖҡиҝҮ Playwright + Canvas Hook жҸҗеҸ–е®Ңж•ҙд№ҰзұҚеҶ…е®№пјҢеҜјеҮәдёә MarkdownгҖӮ

## еҺҹзҗҶ

еҫ®дҝЎиҜ»д№ҰзҪ‘йЎөзүҲдҪҝз”Ё Canvas жёІжҹ“д№ҰзұҚж–Үеӯ—пјҲиҖҢйқһ DOM ж–Үжң¬иҠӮзӮ№пјүпјҢжң¬е·Ҙе…·йҖҡиҝҮд»ҘдёӢжҠҖжңҜж Ҳе®һзҺ°еҶ…е®№жҸҗеҸ–пјҡ

1. **Playwright иҮӘеҠЁеҢ–** вҖ” еҗҜеҠЁ ChromiumпјҢжҢҒд№…еҢ–зҷ»еҪ•дјҡиҜқпјҲжү«з ҒдёҖж¬ЎпјҢеҗҺз»ӯиҮӘеҠЁеӨҚз”Ёпјү
2. **Canvas fillText Hook** вҖ” еңЁйЎөйқў JS жү§иЎҢеүҚжіЁе…Ҙй’©еӯҗпјҢжӢҰжҲӘжүҖжңү `CanvasRenderingContext2D.fillText()` и°ғз”Ё
3. **еҸҢйЎөжӢҶеҲҶ** вҖ” еҫ®дҝЎиҜ»д№ҰеңЁеҗҢдёҖ Canvas дёҠеҗҢж—¶жёІжҹ“еҪ“еүҚйЎөе’ҢдёӢдёҖйЎөпјҲйў„жёІжҹ“дјҳеҢ–пјүпјҢжң¬е·Ҙе…·йҖҡиҝҮжЈҖжөӢ y еқҗж ҮйҮҚзҪ®зӮ№е°ҶдёӨйЎөеӯ—з¬ҰжөҒжӯЈзЎ®еҲҶзҰ»
4. **ж–Үжң¬йҮҚе»ә** вҖ” жҢү (x, y) еқҗж Үе°ҶжҚ•иҺ·зҡ„еҚ•еӯ—з¬ҰйҮҚз»„дёәиЎҢе’Ңж®өиҗҪ
5. **ж јејҸжё…зҗҶ** вҖ” еҗҲе№¶ Canvas жёІжҹ“ж–ӯиЎҢпјҢиҝҳеҺҹиҮӘз„¶ж®өиҗҪ

## е®үиЈ…

```bash
pip install playwright
playwright install chromium
```

## дҪҝз”Ё

```bash
# дј е…Ҙд№ҰзұҚ URL
python weread_export.py https://weread.qq.com/web/bookDetail/xxxxx

# жҲ–зӣҙжҺҘдј  book_id
python weread_export.py dd6324f0813ab9f97g019a24

# жҢҮе®ҡиҫ“еҮәзӣ®еҪ•
python weread_export.py <book_id> -o ./books

# дёҚдҝқеӯҳеҺҹе§Ӣ Canvas ж•°жҚ®пјҲиҠӮзңҒзЈҒзӣҳз©әй—ҙпјү
python weread_export.py <book_id> --no-raw
```

йҰ–ж¬ЎиҝҗиЎҢдјҡеј№еҮәжөҸи§ҲеҷЁзӘ—еҸЈиҰҒжұӮжү«з Ғзҷ»еҪ•еҫ®дҝЎиҜ»д№ҰпјҢзҷ»еҪ•еҗҺдјҡиҜқиҮӘеҠЁдҝқеӯҳеңЁ `cache/browser_profile/`гҖӮ

## иҫ“еҮә

```
output/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ <book_id>/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ meta.json          # д№ҰзұҚе…ғж•°жҚ®пјҲж ҮйўҳгҖҒдҪңиҖ…гҖҒз« иҠӮеҲ—иЎЁпјү
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ chapters/          # жҜҸз« зӢ¬з«Ӣ Markdown
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ 001.md
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ 002.md
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ ...
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ raw/               # еҺҹе§Ӣ Canvas еқҗж Үж•°жҚ®пјҲеҸҜйҖүпјҢз”ЁдәҺи°ғиҜ•/йҮҚеӨ„зҗҶпјү
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ 2.json
в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ ...
в””в”Җв”Җ д№ҰеҗҚ.md                # еҗҲе№¶еҗҺзҡ„е…Ёжң¬ж–Үд»¶
```

## йҷҗеҲ¶

- йңҖиҰҒжңүж•Ҳзҡ„еҫ®дҝЎиҜ»д№ҰиҙҰеҸ·пјҢдё”еҜ№зӣ®ж Үд№ҰзұҚжңүйҳ…иҜ»жқғйҷҗпјҲж— йҷҗеҚЎдјҡе‘ҳжҲ–е·Іиҙӯд№°пјү
- йғЁеҲҶеҮәзүҲзӨҫйҷҗеҲ¶зҪ‘йЎөз«Ҝйҳ…иҜ»пјҲжҳҫзӨә"еҺ» App йҳ…иҜ»"пјүпјҢжӯӨзұ»д№ҰзұҚж— жі•еҜјеҮә
- еҜјеҮәйҖҹеәҰеҸ—зҝ»йЎөзӯүеҫ…ж—¶й—ҙйҷҗеҲ¶пјҢзәҰ 20-30 з§’/з« 

## е·ҘдҪңжөҒзЁӢ

```
жөҸи§ҲеҷЁзҷ»еҪ• вҶ’ иҺ·еҸ–д№ҰзұҚе…ғж•°жҚ® вҶ’ йҖҗз« жү“ејҖ reader йЎөйқў
    вҶ’ жіЁе…Ҙ Canvas Hook вҶ’ зҝ»йЎөж”¶йӣҶ fillText ж•°жҚ®
    вҶ’ еҸҢйЎөжӢҶеҲҶ вҶ’ еқҗж ҮйҮҚе»әж–Үжң¬ вҶ’ ж®өиҗҪж јејҸеҢ– вҶ’ иҫ“еҮә Markdown
```

## еЈ°жҳҺ

д»…дҫӣдёӘдәәеӯҰд№ з ”з©¶дҪҝз”ЁгҖӮиҜ·еӢҝз”ЁдәҺе•Ҷдёҡз”ЁйҖ”жҲ–еӨ§и§„жЁЎдј ж’ӯпјҢиҜ·е°ҠйҮҚи‘—дҪңжқғгҖӮ

xiaohuailabs/xiaohu-video-translate.content:
# xiaohu-video-translate

> дёҖеҸҘиҜқжҠҠеӨ–иҜӯи§Ҷйў‘еҸҳжҲҗ**еёҰдёӯж–Үеӯ—е№•зҡ„и§Ҷйў‘ + дёӯж–Үж–ҮзЁҝ**гҖӮдёӢиҪҪгҖҒиҪ¬еҶҷгҖҒзҝ»иҜ‘гҖҒж¶ҰиүІгҖҒзғ§еҪ•пјҢдёҖжқЎйҫҷи·‘е®ҢпјҢе…ЁзЁӢжң¬ең°пјҢиҪ¬еҶҷдёҚиҠұдёҖеҲҶй’ұ API иҙ№гҖӮ
> One sentence turns a foreign-language video into a **Chinese-subtitled video + a clean transcript**. Download вҶ’ transcribe вҶ’ translate вҶ’ polish вҶ’ burn-in, end to end, all local, zero transcription API cost.

**иҜӯиЁҖ / Language:** [дёӯж–Ү](#дёӯж–Ү) пҪң [English](#english)

иҝҷжҳҜдёҖеҘ— AI зј–зЁӢе‘Ҫд»ӨиЎҢе·Ҙе…·з”Ёзҡ„**жҠҖиғҪпјҲSkillsпјү**гҖӮе®ғжң¬иҙЁжҳҜгҖҢи„ҡжң¬ + дёҖд»ҪиҜҙжҳҺд№ҰгҖҚпјҢ**дёҚз»‘жӯ»д»»дҪ•дёҖе®¶**пјҡ[Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)гҖҒOpenClawпјҲе°ҸйҫҷиҷҫпјүгҖҒGemini CLIгҖҒCodex йғҪиғҪз”ЁгҖӮиЈ…еҘҪеҗҺдҪ дёҚз”Ёи®°д»»дҪ•е‘Ҫд»ӨпјҢзӣҙжҺҘиҜҙдёҖеҸҘгҖҢжҠҠиҝҷдёӘй“ҫжҺҘзҝ»иҜ‘жҲҗдёӯж–Үеӯ—е№•и§Ҷйў‘гҖҚпјҢеү©дёӢзҡ„е®ғе…ЁиҮӘеҠЁеҒҡе®ҢгҖӮ

---

## дёӯж–Ү

### дёҖжқЎйҫҷе·ҘдҪңжөҒ

е®ғжҠҠеҺҹжң¬иҰҒејҖеӣӣдә”дёӘиҪҜд»¶гҖҒжқҘеӣһжҠҳи…ҫдёҖдёӨдёӘе°Ҹж—¶зҡ„жҙ»пјҢдёІжҲҗдёҖжқЎе…ЁиҮӘеҠЁжөҒж°ҙзәҝпјҡ

```
и§Ҷйў‘й“ҫжҺҘ/жң¬ең°ж–Үд»¶
      в”Ӯ
      в–ј
  в‘  дёӢиҪҪ  в”Җв”Җв–ә  в‘Ў жҸҗеҸ–йҹійў‘ + Whisper иҪ¬еҶҷ  в”Җв”Җв–ә  в‘ў зҝ»иҜ‘  в”Җв”Җв–ә  в‘Ј ж¶ҰиүІ  в”Җв”Җв–ә  в‘Ө зғ§еҪ•еӯ—е№•
      в”Ӯ                  пјҲиҜҚзә§ж—¶й—ҙжҲіпјү          пјҲд»»ж„ҸеӨ–иҜӯвҶ’дёӯж–Үпјү  пјҲж–ӯеҸҘ/еҺ»ж ҮзӮ№/еҜ№иҪҙпјү      в”Ӯ
      в”Ӯ                                                                                в–ј
      в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә  еёҰдёӯж–Үеӯ—е№•зҡ„и§Ҷйў‘ + Markdown ж–ҮзЁҝ
```

дҪ еҸӘз®ЎиҜҙдёҖеҸҘиҜқпјҢдә”жӯҘе®ғиҮӘе·ұиө°е®ҢгҖӮдёӯй—ҙд»»дҪ•дёҖжӯҘиҰҒи°ғж•ҙпјҲеҸҢиҜӯгҖҒдёҚиҰҒж°ҙеҚ°гҖҒеҝ«йҖҹжЁЎејҸпјүпјҢеҜ№е®ғиҜҙе°ұиЎҢгҖӮ

### ж”ҜжҢҒеӨҡиҜӯз§Қ

дёҚжҢ‘иҜӯиЁҖгҖӮ**иӢұиҜӯгҖҒж—ҘиҜӯгҖҒйҹ©иҜӯгҖҒжі•иҜӯгҖҒиҘҝзҸӯзүҷиҜӯвҖҰвҖҰеҸӘиҰҒ Whisper еҗ¬еҫ—жҮӮзҡ„пјҢйғҪиғҪиҪ¬жҲҗдёӯж–Үеӯ—е№•гҖӮ** Whisper иҮӘеҠЁиҜҶеҲ«еҺҹиҜӯз§ҚпјҢзҝ»иҜ‘зҺҜиҠӮжҠҠд»»ж„ҸеӨ–иҜӯзҝ»жҲҗдёӯж–ҮгҖӮдёӯж–Үи§Ҷйў‘еҲҷеҸӘеҒҡиҪ¬еҶҷе’Ңж–ҮзЁҝпјҢдёҚиө°зҝ»иҜ‘гҖӮ

### еӯ—е№•дёӨз§ҚйҖү

- **зәҜдёӯж–Ү**пјҡз”»йқўе№ІеҮҖ
- **дёӯиӢұеҸҢиҜӯ**пјҡдёӯж–ҮеӨ§гҖҒиӢұж–Үе°ҸпјҢдё»ж¬ЎеҲҶжҳҺпјҲз”ЁзңҹжӯЈзҡ„ ASS еӯ—е№•еҒҡеӯ—еҸ·еҸҚе·®пјҢSRT еҒҡдёҚеҲ°иҝҷдёӘпјүпјҢйҖӮеҗҲйЎәдҫҝз»ғеҗ¬еҠӣ

### дёәд»Җд№Ҳз”Ёе®ғ

- **жң¬ең°гҖҒе…Қиҙ№гҖҒиғҪзҰ»зәҝ**пјҡиҪ¬еҶҷз”Ё [Whisper](https://github.com/openai/whisper)пјҲиӢ№жһңиҠҜзүҮиө° MLX + Metal GPU еҠ йҖҹпјүпјҢеңЁдҪ з”өи„‘йҮҢе®ҢжҲҗпјҢдёҚдёҠдј гҖҒдёҚж”¶иҙ№гҖӮзҝ»иҜ‘еӨҚз”ЁдҪ е·Із»ҸеңЁз”Ёзҡ„ AIпјҢдёҚз”ЁеҶҚеҚ•зӢ¬д№°зҝ»иҜ‘ APIгҖӮ
- **ж—¶й—ҙжҲіжҳҜзңҹзҡ„еҮҶ**пјҡжӢҝиҜҚзә§ж—¶й—ҙжҲіжҢүгҖҢеҸҘеӯҗ + еҒңйЎҝгҖҚеҲҮеҲҶпјҢеӯ—е№•дёҚдјҡи·‘еңЁиҜҙиҜқдәәеүҚйқўпјҢд№ҹдёҚдјҡеҚҠеҸҘз”©еҲ°дёӢдёҖжқЎгҖӮ
- **еӯ—е№•жҳҜз»ҷдәәзңӢзҡ„**пјҢдёҚжҳҜжңәзҝ»зӣҙеҮәпјҡиҮӘеҠЁзә жӯЈиҪ¬еҶҷеҗ¬й”ҷзҡ„дё“жңүеҗҚиҜҚпјҲClaude еёёиў«еҗ¬жҲҗ cloudгҖҒMCP иў«еҗ¬жҲҗ NCPпјүпјҢжҢүиҜӯд№үж–ӯеҸҘпјҢжңҜиҜӯдҝқз•ҷиӢұж–ҮгҖӮ
- **зғ§еӯ—е№• + ж°ҙеҚ°дёҖж¬Ўзј–з Ғе®ҢжҲҗ**пјҢдёҚжҺүз”»иҙЁгҖӮ

### е®ғе…¶е®һжҳҜдёүдёӘжҠҖиғҪ

| жҠҖиғҪ | иҒҢиҙЈ |
|------|------|
| **xiaohu-video-md** | жҖ»жҢҮжҢҘгҖӮдёӢиҪҪ / жҸҗйҹійў‘ / Whisper иҪ¬еҶҷ / и°ғз”Ёж¶ҰиүІ / зғ§еӯ—е№• / еҮә Markdown |
| **xiaohu-subtitle-polish** | еӯ—е№•зҝ»иҜ‘дёҺж¶ҰиүІгҖӮзә й”ҷгҖҒзҝ»иҜ‘гҖҒж–ӯеҸҘгҖҒеҺ»ж ҮзӮ№гҖҒж—¶й—ҙжҲіеҜ№йҪҗгҖҒеҸҢиҜӯ ASS |
| **xiaohu-video-download** | зәҜдёӢиҪҪе·Ҙе…·гҖӮдёӢи§Ҷйў‘ / дёӢйҹійў‘ / дёӢж’ӯж”ҫеҲ—иЎЁ / з»ҷжң¬ең°и§Ҷйў‘зғ§еӯ—е№• |

зҝ»иҜ‘з®Ўзәҝз”ұ `xiaohu-video-md` жҖ»и°ғеәҰпјҢзҝ»иҜ‘йӮЈдёҖжӯҘе®ғиҮӘе·ұдјҡеҺ»еҸ« `xiaohu-subtitle-polish`гҖӮдёүдёӘжҠҖиғҪеҗ„иҮӘзӢ¬з«ӢпјҢд№ҹеҸҜеҚ•зӢ¬з”ЁгҖӮ

### жј”зӨәжЎҲдҫӢ

еҗҢдёҖж®ө a16z иӢұж–Үи®ҝи°ҲпјҢзҝ»жҲҗдёӯ / ж—Ҙ / йҹ© / йҳҝ / жі• 5 з§ҚиҜӯиЁҖзҡ„еҸҢиҜӯеӯ—е№•вҖ”вҖ”еҗ„иҜӯз§ҚиҜ‘ж–ҮеңЁдёҠгҖҒиӢұж–ҮеҺҹж–ҮеңЁдёӢпјҢд»ҺеҸіеҫҖе·Ұд№ҰеҶҷзҡ„йҳҝжӢүдјҜиҜӯд№ҹжҺ’еҫ—ж•ҙж•ҙйҪҗйҪҗпјҡ

![еӨҡиҜӯиЁҖеҸҢиҜӯеӯ—е№•еҜ№жҜ”](assets/demo-multilang.jpg)

зғ§иҝӣз”»йқўеҗҺзҡ„дёӯиӢұеҸҢиҜӯеӯ—е№•пјҲдёӯж–ҮеӨ§гҖҒиӢұж–Үе°ҸпјҢиҙҙеә•дёҚжҢЎдәәпјүпјҡ

![дёӯиӢұеҸҢиҜӯеӯ—е№•е®һжӢҚ](assets/demo-bilingual.jpg)

### ж”ҜжҢҒе“Әдәӣ AI зј–зЁӢе·Ҙе…·

ж ёеҝғжҳҜи„ҡжң¬ + иҜҙжҳҺд№ҰпјҲжҜҸдёӘжҠҖиғҪзҡ„ `SKILL.md`пјүпјҢ**д»»дҪ•иғҪиҜ»жҠҖиғҪ/жҢҮд»Өж–Үд»¶гҖҒеҸҲиғҪи·‘е‘Ҫд»Өзҡ„ AI зј–зЁӢе·Ҙе…·йғҪиғҪй©ұеҠЁе®ғ**гҖӮеҗ„е®¶еҠ иҪҪж–№ејҸдёҚеҗҢпјҡ

- **Claude Code**пјҡеҺҹз”ҹжҠҖиғҪгҖӮ`install.sh` зӣҙжҺҘжҠҠдёүдёӘжҠҖиғҪиЈ…иҝӣ `~/.claude/skills/`
- **OpenClawпјҲе°Ҹйҫҷиҷҫпјү**пјҡеҗҢж ·жҳҜжҠҖиғҪеҲ¶пјҢжҢүе®ғзҡ„ `openclaw skills` ж–№ејҸиЈ…е…ҘеҚіеҸҜ
- **Gemini CLI**пјҡд»“еә“иҮӘеёҰ `gemini-extension.json`пјҢдҪңдёә extension еҠ иҪҪ
- **Codex зӯүе…¶д»–**пјҡжҠҠеҜ№еә”жҠҖиғҪзҡ„ `SKILL.md` е–Ӯз»ҷе®ғеҪ“и§„еҲҷпјҢи®©е®ғз…§зқҖи°ғз”Ё `scripts/` йҮҢзҡ„и„ҡжң¬пјӣжҲ–иҖ…зӣҙжҺҘжүӢеҠЁи·‘и„ҡжң¬

дёӢйқўзҡ„е®үиЈ…жӯҘйӘӨд»Ҙ Claude Code дёәдҫӢпјҢе…¶д»–е·Ҙе…·жҠҠ"еӨҚеҲ¶еҲ° `~/.claude/skills/`"жҚўжҲҗеҗ„иҮӘзҡ„жҠҖиғҪзӣ®еҪ•еҚіеҸҜгҖӮ

### е®үиЈ…

#### дёҖгҖҒmacOSпјҲжңҖйЎәпјҢе°Өе…¶жҳҜ Apple Siliconпјү

```bash
# 1. еҹәзЎҖе·Ҙе…·пјҲжІЎиЈ… Homebrew е…ҲеҺ» brew.sh иЈ…пјү
brew install yt-dlp ffmpeg

# 2. иҪ¬еҶҷеј•ж“ҺпјҲApple Silicon йҖү mlx-whisperпјҢиө° GPU еҠ йҖҹжңҖеҝ«пјү
pip3 install --break-system-packages mlx-whisper
# йқһ Apple Silicon зҡ„ Mac з”Ёе…ңеә•еј•ж“Һпјҡ
# pip3 install --break-system-packages faster-whisper

# 3. иЈ…жҠҖиғҪ
git clone https://github.com/xiaohuailabs/xiaohu-video-translate.git
cd xiaohu-video-translate
bash install.sh
```

з„¶еҗҺеңЁ `~/.claude/skills/xiaohu-video-md/config.json` жҠҠ `output_dir` ж”№жҲҗдҪ зҡ„з»қеҜ№и·Ҝеҫ„гҖӮMLX жЁЎеһӢйҰ–ж¬ЎиҝҗиЎҢдјҡиҮӘеҠЁд»Һ HuggingFaвҖҰ

cellebrite-labs/ghidra-rpc.content:
# ghidra-rpc

An agentic skill that gives an LLM access to [Ghidra](https://ghidra-sre.org/) so
it can perform reverse engineering tasks autonomously: decompile functions, trace
call graphs, rename and annotate symbols, define data structures, diff binary
versions, and more вҖ” all without human intervention.

`ghidra-rpc` runs Ghidra as a persistent background daemon and exposes its
capabilities through a CLI that returns structured JSON. Any AI coding assistant
that can run shell commands (pi, Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) can drive a full RE
session by issuing commands and reasoning over the results.

Developed at **[Cellebrite Labs](https://github.com/cellebrite-labs)**.

## What the AI Can Do

| Area | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|
| **Understand code** | Decompile functions to pseudo-C, disassemble, inspect CFG and P-code |
| **Navigate** | Trace callers/callees, search strings and byte patterns, find cross-references |
| **Annotate** | Rename functions and symbols, add comments, set bookmarks and tags |
| **Type recovery** | Define structs/unions/enums, retype variables, set function signatures |
| **Patch** | Assemble instructions (SLEIGH), write raw bytes, override flow types |
| **Diff binaries** | Version-track two builds, diff changed functions, match functions via BSim |

## Quick Start

```bash
# Prerequisites: Ghidra 11+, Python 3.11+, Java 17+, uv
export GHIDRA_INSTALL_DIR=/opt/ghidra_12.0
uv tool install /path/to/ghidra-rpc
```

Once installed, tell your AI assistant to start a session:

> *"Load /usr/bin/ls into Ghidra and find any unsafe string operations."*

For manual use or debugging:

```bash
# Start the daemon (use --detach for background)
ghidra-rpc start --project /tmp/work.gpr --headless

export GHIDRA_RPC_PROJECT=/tmp/work.gpr
ghidra-rpc load /usr/bin/ls
ghidra-rpc decompile ls main
ghidra-rpc xrefs-to ls strcmp
ghidra-rpc rename-function ls FUN_00401234 parse_args
```

See [docs/install.md](docs/install.md) for prerequisites and
[docs/quickstart.md](docs/quickstart.md) for a full walkthrough.

## How It Works

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ      Unix Socket       в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  LLM agent  в”Ӯ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ JSON/newline в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ в”Ӯ  ghidra-rpc daemon       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  (via CLI)  в”Ӯ  вҶҗв”Җв”Җ JSON/newline в”Җв”Җв”Җ  в”Ӯ  (PyGhidra + Ghidra JVM) в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                        в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

The daemon runs Ghidra in-process via
[PyGhidra](https://github.com/NationalSecurityAgency/ghidra/tree/master/Ghidra/Features/PyGhidra).
Ghidra loads the binary once and stays warm between commands вҖ” no re-analysis on
every invocation. All changes (renames, comments, type definitions, patches) are
saved to the Ghidra project after every command and remain visible when you open the
project in the Ghidra GUI.

## Documentation

- [Installation](docs/install.md)
- [Quick Start](docs/quickstart.md)
- [Troubleshooting](docs/troubleshooting.md)
- [Internals](docs/internals.md) вҖ” session/socket design, Ghidra API notes

### Workflow Guides
вҖҰ

# Binance-Deribit BTC и·ЁжүҖеҘ—еҲ©иҮӘеҠЁеҢ–дәӨжҳ“зЁӢеәҸ

иҝҷжҳҜдёҖдёӘйқўеҗ‘ Deribit жңҹжқғдёҺ Binance USDT-M ж°ёз»ӯеҗҲзәҰзҡ„и·ЁдәӨжҳ“жүҖеҘ—еҲ©/еҜ№еҶІйЎ№зӣ®гҖӮйЎ№зӣ®еҢ…еҗ«еҘ—еҲ©жү«жҸҸгҖҒдёӢеҚ•жү§иЎҢгҖҒжҢҒд»“зӣ‘жҺ§гҖҒз»“з®—еӨ„зҗҶгҖҒRedis зҠ¶жҖҒжҒўеӨҚгҖҒTelegram е‘ҠиӯҰдёҺ Web зӣ‘жҺ§йқўжқҝгҖӮ

> йЈҺйҷ©жҸҗзӨәпјҡжң¬йЎ№зӣ®ж¶үеҸҠдәӨжҳ“жүҖ API е’ҢиҮӘеҠЁдәӨжҳ“йҖ»иҫ‘гҖӮиҜ·е…ҲдҪҝз”ЁжөӢиҜ•зҪ‘гҖҒе°Ҹиө„йҮ‘гҖҒеҸӘиҜ»жҲ–дҪҺжқғйҷҗ API Key еҒҡе……еҲҶйӘҢиҜҒгҖӮд»»дҪ•иҮӘеҠЁдәӨжҳ“зӯ–з•ҘйғҪеҸҜиғҪеӣ зҪ‘з»ңгҖҒдәӨжҳ“жүҖжҺҘеҸЈгҖҒиЎҢжғ…жіўеҠЁгҖҒй…ҚзҪ®й”ҷиҜҜжҲ–д»Јз Ғзјәйҷ·йҖ жҲҗдәҸжҚҹгҖӮ

е®һж—¶дәӨжөҒе№іеҸ°пјҡhttps://linux.do/t/topic/2323516

## еҠҹиғҪжҰӮи§Ҳ

- жү«жҸҸ Deribit BTC жңҹжқғдёҺ Binance ж°ёз»ӯеҗҲзәҰд№Ӣй—ҙзҡ„жҪңеңЁеҘ—еҲ©жңәдјҡгҖӮ
- дҪҝз”Ё Redis дҝқеӯҳиҝҗиЎҢзҠ¶жҖҒпјҢж”ҜжҢҒиҝӣзЁӢйҮҚеҗҜеҗҺзҡ„зҠ¶жҖҒжҒўеӨҚгҖӮ
- дҪҝз”Ё SQLite и®°еҪ•дәӨжҳ“гҖҒиҙҰжҲ·жқғзӣҠе’Ңжө®дәҸеі°еҖјзӯүиҝҗиЎҢж•°жҚ®гҖӮ
- ж”ҜжҢҒ Telegram жңәеҷЁдәәжҹҘзңӢзҠ¶жҖҒгҖҒжҡӮеҒңгҖҒжҒўеӨҚгҖҒи°ғж•ҙйғЁеҲҶеҸӮж•°е’ҢжҺҘж”¶е‘ҠиӯҰгҖӮ
- жҸҗдҫӣ Flask Web зӣ‘жҺ§йқўжқҝжҹҘзңӢжҢҒд»“гҖҒж—Ҙеҝ—гҖҒз»ҹи®Ўе’ҢиҙҰжҲ·жқғзӣҠгҖӮ
- еҗҜеҠЁеүҚжү§иЎҢ preflight жЈҖжҹҘпјҢзЎ®и®Ө `.env`гҖҒAPI KeyгҖҒRedisгҖҒдәӨжҳ“жүҖ RESTгҖҒжҢҒд»“е’ҢзЈҒзӣҳз©әй—ҙгҖӮ
- й»ҳи®Өй…ҚзҪ®дёәжөӢиҜ•зҪ‘пјҢе®һзӣҳеүҚйңҖиҰҒжҳҺзЎ®дҝ®ж”№й…ҚзҪ®е№¶йҮҚж–°жЈҖжҹҘйЈҺжҺ§еҸӮж•°гҖӮ

## зӣ®еҪ•з»“жһ„

```text
.
в”ңв”Җв”Җ binance_deribit.py        # дё»зЁӢеәҸе…ҘеҸЈ
в”ңв”Җв”Җ binance-monitor.py        # Web зӣ‘жҺ§йқўжқҝ
в”ңв”Җв”Җ config.py                 # зӯ–з•ҘгҖҒдәӨжҳ“жүҖгҖҒйЈҺжҺ§й…ҚзҪ®
в”ңв”Җв”Җ binance_futures.py        # Binance жңҹиҙ§жҺҘеҸЈ
в”ңв”Җв”Җ deribit_client.py         # Deribit WebSocket/REST е®ўжҲ·з«Ҝ
в”ңв”Җв”Җ trade_executor.py         # дёӢеҚ•жү§иЎҢйҖ»иҫ‘
в”ңв”Җв”Җ telegram_handler.py       # Telegram е‘Ҫд»Өе’Ңе‘ҠиӯҰ
в”ңв”Җв”Җ db_store.py               # SQLite жҢҒд№…еҢ–
в”ңв”Җв”Җ engine/                   # ж ёеҝғеј•ж“Һ mixin жЁЎеқ—
в”ңв”Җв”Җ .env.example              # зҺҜеўғеҸҳйҮҸжЁЎжқҝ
в”ңв”Җв”Җ requirements.txt          # Python дҫқиө–
в”ңв”Җв”Җ pipinstall.txt            # еҺҹе§Ӣе®үиЈ…е‘Ҫд»ӨеӨҮеҝҳ
в””в”Җв”Җ README.md
```

## йғЁзҪІзҺҜеўғиҰҒжұӮ

жҺЁиҚҗзҺҜеўғпјҡ

- Python 3.10 жҲ–жӣҙй«ҳзүҲжң¬
- Redis 6 жҲ–жӣҙй«ҳзүҲжң¬
- macOS жҲ– Ubuntu Linux
- еҸҜи®ҝй—® DeribitгҖҒBinance FuturesгҖҒTelegram API зҡ„зҪ‘з»ңзҺҜеўғ

Python дҫқиө–пјҡ

- `aiohttp`
- `flask`
- `orjson`
- `redis`
- `requests`
- `websockets`

## еҝ«йҖҹйғЁзҪІ

### 1. е…ӢйҡҶд»Јз Ғ

```bash
git clone https://github.com/beijingcao/binance-deribit-btc.git
cd binance-deribit-btc
```

### 2. еҲӣе»ә Python иҷҡжӢҹзҺҜеўғ

macOS / Linuxпјҡ

```bash
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install --upgrade pip
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

еҰӮжһңжңҚеҠЎеҷЁеҗҢж—¶е®үиЈ…дәҶеӨҡдёӘ Python зүҲжң¬пјҢиҜ·зЎ®и®Ө `python --version` жҢҮеҗ‘дҪ еҮҶеӨҮиҝҗиЎҢйЎ№зӣ®зҡ„зүҲжң¬гҖӮ

### 3. е®үиЈ…е№¶еҗҜеҠЁ Redis

macOS Homebrewпјҡ

```bash
brew install redis
brew services start redis
redis-cli ping
```

Ubuntu / Debianпјҡ

```bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y redis-server
sudo systemctl enable redis-server
sudo systemctl start redis-server
redis-cli ping
```

еҰӮжһңиҝ”еӣһ `PONG`пјҢиҜҙжҳҺ Redis е·ІжӯЈеёёиҝҗиЎҢгҖӮ

### 4. й…ҚзҪ®зҺҜеўғеҸҳйҮҸ

еӨҚеҲ¶жЁЎжқҝпјҡ

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

зј–иҫ‘ `.env`пјҡ

```bash
nano .env
```

йңҖиҰҒеЎ«еҶҷзҡ„еӯ—ж®өпјҡ

```bash
TG_BOT_TOKEN=
TG_CHAT_ID=
